LocalScriptWithBastionTunneling.description:
#### A local script with secure tunneling through a bastion host.

---

This script type runs locally but tunnels connections to resources through a bastion server.
It provides a secure, encrypted connection to resources that are only accessible within the VPC,
such as private databases or Redis clusters.

The environment variables injected by `connectTo` are automatically adjusted to use the tunneled endpoints.

_MdxDesc.sd: A local script with secure tunneling through a bastion host.
_MdxDesc.ld:
This script type runs locally but tunnels connections to resources through a bastion server. It provides a secure, encrypted connection to resources that are only accessible within the VPC, such as private databases or Redis clusters.

The environment variables injected by connectTo are automatically adjusted to use the tunneled endpoints.

bastionResource.description:
#### Bastion Resource Name

---

The name of the bastion resource to use for tunneling to protected resources.

_MdxDesc.sd: Bastion Resource Name
_MdxDesc.ld: The name of the bastion resource to use for tunneling to protected resources.

executeScript.description:
#### Execute Script

---

The path to a script file to execute. The script can be written in JavaScript, TypeScript, or Python and runs in a separate process.

The executable is determined by `defaults:configure` or the system default (`node` for JS/TS, `python` for Python). You can only use one of `executeScript`, `executeScripts`, `executeCommand`, or `executeCommands`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Execute Script
_MdxDesc.ld:
The path to a script file to execute. The script can be written in JavaScript, TypeScript, or Python and runs in a separate process.

The executable is determined by defaults:configure or the system default (node for JS/TS, python for Python). You can only use one of executeScript, executeScripts, executeCommand, or executeCommands.

executeCommand.description:
#### Execute Command

---

A single terminal command to execute in a separate shell process.

The command runs on the machine executing the Stacktape command. Be aware of potential differences between local and CI environments (e.g., OS, shell). You can only use one of `executeScript`, `executeScripts`, `executeCommand`, or `executeCommands`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Execute Command
_MdxDesc.ld:
A single terminal command to execute in a separate shell process.

The command runs on the machine executing the Stacktape command. Be aware of potential differences between local and CI environments (e.g., OS, shell). You can only use one of executeScript, executeScripts, executeCommand, or executeCommands.

executeScripts.description:
#### Execute Scripts

---

A list of script files to execute sequentially. Each script runs in a separate process.

The script can be written in JavaScript, TypeScript, or Python. The executable is determined by `defaults:configure` or the system default. You can only use one of `executeScript`, `executeScripts`, `executeCommand`, or `executeCommands`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Execute Scripts
_MdxDesc.ld:
A list of script files to execute sequentially. Each script runs in a separate process.

The script can be written in JavaScript, TypeScript, or Python. The executable is determined by defaults:configure or the system default. You can only use one of executeScript, executeScripts, executeCommand, or executeCommands.

executeCommands.description:
#### Execute Commands

---

A list of terminal commands to execute sequentially. Each command runs in a separate shell process.

The commands run on the machine executing the Stacktape command. Be aware of potential differences between environments. You can only use one of `executeScript`, `executeScripts`, `executeCommand`, or `executeCommands`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Execute Commands
_MdxDesc.ld:
A list of terminal commands to execute sequentially. Each command runs in a separate shell process.

The commands run on the machine executing the Stacktape command. Be aware of potential differences between environments. You can only use one of executeScript, executeScripts, executeCommand, or executeCommands.

cwd.description:
#### Working Directory

---

The directory where the script or command will be executed.
cwd.default: The directory where the Stacktape command was executed.

_MdxDesc.sd: Working Directory
_MdxDesc.ld: The directory where the script or command will be executed.

pipeStdio.description:
#### Pipe Stdio

---

If `true`, pipes the standard input/output (stdio) of the hook process to the main process. This allows you to see logs from your hook and interact with prompts.

_MdxDesc.sd: Pipe Stdio
_MdxDesc.ld: If true, pipes the standard input/output (stdio) of the hook process to the main process. This allows you to see logs from your hook and interact with prompts.

connectTo.description:
#### Connect To

---

A list of resources the script needs to interact with. Stacktape automatically injects environment variables with connection details for each specified resource.

Environment variable names are in the format `STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[VARIABLE_NAME]` (e.g., `STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING`).

**Injected Variables by Resource Type:**
- **`Bucket`**: `NAME`, `ARN`
- **`DynamoDbTable`**: `NAME`, `ARN`, `STREAM_ARN`
- **`MongoDbAtlasCluster`**: `CONNECTION_STRING`
- **`RelationalDatabase`**: `CONNECTION_STRING`, `JDBC_CONNECTION_STRING`, `HOST`, `PORT`. For Aurora clusters, `READER_CONNECTION_STRING`, `READER_JDBC_CONNECTION_STRING`, and `READER_HOST` are also included.
- **`RedisCluster`**: `HOST`, `READER_HOST`, `PORT`
- **`EventBus`**: `ARN`
- **`Function`**: `ARN`
- **`BatchJob`**: `JOB_DEFINITION_ARN`, `STATE_MACHINE_ARN`
- **`UserAuthPool`**: `ID`, `CLIENT_ID`, `ARN`
- **`SnsTopic`**: `ARN`, `NAME`
- **`SqsQueue`**: `ARN`, `NAME`, `URL`
- **`UpstashKafkaTopic`**: `TOPIC_NAME`, `TOPIC_ID`, `USERNAME`, `PASSWORD`, `TCP_ENDPOINT`, `REST_URL`
- **`UpstashRedis`**: `HOST`, `PORT`, `PASSWORD`, `REST_TOKEN`, `REST_URL`, `REDIS_URL`
- **`PrivateService`**: `ADDRESS`
- **`WebService`**: `URL`

_MdxDesc.sd: Connect To
_MdxDesc.ld:
A list of resources the script needs to interact with. Stacktape automatically injects environment variables with connection details for each specified resource.

Environment variable names are in the format STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[VARIABLE_NAME] (e.g., STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING).

Injected Variables by Resource Type:

  • Bucket: NAME, ARN
  • DynamoDbTable: NAME, ARN, STREAM_ARN
  • MongoDbAtlasCluster: CONNECTION_STRING
  • RelationalDatabase: CONNECTION_STRING, JDBC_CONNECTION_STRING, HOST, PORT. For Aurora clusters, READER_CONNECTION_STRING, READER_JDBC_CONNECTION_STRING, and READER_HOST are also included.
  • RedisCluster: HOST, READER_HOST, PORT
  • EventBus: ARN
  • Function: ARN
  • BatchJob: JOB_DEFINITION_ARN, STATE_MACHINE_ARN
  • UserAuthPool: ID, CLIENT_ID, ARN
  • SnsTopic: ARN, NAME
  • SqsQueue: ARN, NAME, URL
  • UpstashKafkaTopic: TOPIC_NAME, TOPIC_ID, USERNAME, PASSWORD, TCP_ENDPOINT, REST_URL
  • UpstashRedis: HOST, PORT, PASSWORD, REST_TOKEN, REST_URL, REDIS_URL
  • PrivateService: ADDRESS
  • WebService: URL

environment.description:
#### Environment Variables

---

A list of environment variables to pass to the script or command.

Values can be:
- A static string, number, or boolean.
- The result of a [custom directive](https://docs.stacktape.com/configuration/directives/#custom-directives).
- A reference to another resource's parameter using the [`$ResourceParam` directive](https://docs.stacktape.com/configuration/referencing-parameters/).
- A value from a [secret](https://docs.stacktape.com/resources/secrets/) using the [`$Secret` directive](https://docs.stacktape.com/configuration/directives/#secret).

_MdxDesc.sd: Environment Variables
_MdxDesc.ld:
A list of environment variables to pass to the script or command.

Values can be:

  • A static string, number, or boolean.
  • The result of a custom directive.
  • A reference to another resource's parameter using the `$ResourceParam` directive.
  • A value from a secret using the `$Secret` directive.

assumeRoleOfResource.description:
#### Assume Role of Resource

---

The name of a deployed resource whose IAM role the script should assume. This grants the script the same permissions as the specified resource.

The resource must be deployed before the script is executed. Stacktape injects temporary AWS credentials as environment variables, which are automatically used by most AWS SDKs and CLIs.

**Supported Resource Types:**
- `function`
- `batch-job`
- `worker-service`
- `web-service`
- `private-service`
- `multi-container-workload`
- `nextjs-web`
- `astro-web`
- `nuxt-web`
- `sveltekit-web`
- `solidstart-web`
- `tanstack-web`
- `remix-web`

_MdxDesc.sd: Assume Role of Resource
_MdxDesc.ld:
The name of a deployed resource whose IAM role the script should assume. This grants the script the same permissions as the specified resource.

The resource must be deployed before the script is executed. Stacktape injects temporary AWS credentials as environment variables, which are automatically used by most AWS SDKs and CLIs.

Supported Resource Types:

  • function
  • batch-job
  • worker-service
  • web-service
  • private-service
  • multi-container-workload
  • nextjs-web
  • astro-web
  • nuxt-web
  • sveltekit-web
  • solidstart-web
  • tanstack-web
  • remix-web

name.description:
#### Directive Name

---

The name of the custom directive.

_MdxDesc.sd: Directive Name
_MdxDesc.ld: The name of the custom directive.

filePath.description:
#### File Path

---

The path to the file where the directive is defined, in the format `{file-path}:{handler}`.

If the `{handler}` is omitted:
- For `.js` and `.ts` files, the `default` export is used.
- For `.py` files, the `main` function is used.

_MdxDesc.sd: File Path
_MdxDesc.ld:
The path to the file where the directive is defined, in the format {file-path}:{handler}.

If the {handler} is omitted:

  • For .js and .ts files, the default export is used.
  • For .py files, the main function is used.

terminationProtection.description:
#### Prevents accidental stack deletion. Must be disabled before you can delete.

---

Recommended for production stacks. To delete a protected stack, first deploy with
`terminationProtection: false`, then run the delete command.

_MdxDesc.sd: Prevents accidental stack deletion. Must be disabled before you can delete.
_MdxDesc.ld: Recommended for production stacks. To delete a protected stack, first deploy with terminationProtection: false, then run the delete command.

cloudformationRoleArn.description:
#### IAM role for CloudFormation to assume during create/update/delete operations.

---

Use this when your deploy user has limited permissions and CloudFormation needs
a more privileged role to manage resources. The role is persisted across deployments
and reused for delete/rollback even if removed from config later.

_MdxDesc.sd: IAM role for CloudFormation to assume during create/update/delete operations.
_MdxDesc.ld: Use this when your deploy user has limited permissions and CloudFormation needs a more privileged role to manage resources. The role is persisted across deployments and reused for delete/rollback even if removed from config later.

triggerRollbackOnAlarms.description:
#### Alarms that trigger automatic rollback if they fire during deployment.

---

Specify alarm names (from `alarms` section) or ARNs. The alarm must already exist -
a newly created alarm only takes effect on the *next* deployment.

Use with `monitoringTimeAfterDeploymentInMinutes` to keep watching after deploy completes.

_MdxDesc.sd: Alarms that trigger automatic rollback if they fire during deployment.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Specify alarm names (from alarms section) or ARNs. The alarm must already exist - a newly created alarm only takes effect on the next deployment.

Use with monitoringTimeAfterDeploymentInMinutes to keep watching after deploy completes.

monitoringTimeAfterDeploymentInMinutes.description:
#### How long (in minutes) to monitor rollback alarms after deployment completes.

---

If an alarm fires during this window, the stack rolls back automatically.
Only useful when `triggerRollbackOnAlarms` is configured.

_MdxDesc.sd: How long (in minutes) to monitor rollback alarms after deployment completes.
_MdxDesc.ld: If an alarm fires during this window, the stack rolls back automatically. Only useful when triggerRollbackOnAlarms is configured.

disableAutoRollback.description:
#### Keep the stack in a failed state instead of rolling back on deployment failure.

---

Useful for debugging: inspect what went wrong, then fix and redeploy
(or run `stacktape rollback` manually). By default, failed deployments
auto-rollback to the last working state.

_MdxDesc.sd: Keep the stack in a failed state instead of rolling back on deployment failure.
_MdxDesc.ld: Useful for debugging: inspect what went wrong, then fix and redeploy (or run stacktape rollback manually). By default, failed deployments auto-rollback to the last working state.

publishEventsToArn.description:
#### SNS topic ARNs to receive CloudFormation stack events during deployment.

---

Useful for monitoring deployments in external systems (Slack, PagerDuty, etc.).

_MdxDesc.sd: SNS topic ARNs to receive CloudFormation stack events during deployment.
_MdxDesc.ld: Useful for monitoring deployments in external systems (Slack, PagerDuty, etc.).

previousVersionsToKeep.description:
#### How many old deployment artifacts (Lambda bundles, container images) to keep.

---

Older versions are cleaned up automatically. Lower values save storage costs,
higher values make it easier to roll back to previous versions.

_MdxDesc.sd: How many old deployment artifacts (Lambda bundles, container images) to keep.
_MdxDesc.ld: Older versions are cleaned up automatically. Lower values save storage costs, higher values make it easier to roll back to previous versions.

disableS3TransferAcceleration.description:
#### Disable faster uploads via S3 Transfer Acceleration.

---

Transfer Acceleration routes uploads through the nearest AWS edge location
for faster deploys, especially from distant regions. Adds a small cost per GB.
Automatically disabled in regions where it's not available.

_MdxDesc.sd: Disable faster uploads via S3 Transfer Acceleration.
_MdxDesc.ld: Transfer Acceleration routes uploads through the nearest AWS edge location for faster deploys, especially from distant regions. Adds a small cost per GB. Automatically disabled in regions where it's not available.

outputs.description:
#### Custom values to display and save after each deployment.

---

Use outputs to surface dynamic values like API URLs, database endpoints, or resource ARNs
that are only known after deployment. Outputs are:
- Printed in the terminal after deploy
- Saved to the stack info JSON file
- Optionally exported for cross-stack references (via `export: true`)

_MdxDesc.sd: Custom values to display and save after each deployment.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use outputs to surface dynamic values like API URLs, database endpoints, or resource ARNs that are only known after deployment. Outputs are:

  • Printed in the terminal after deploy
  • Saved to the stack info JSON file
  • Optionally exported for cross-stack references (via export: true)

tags.description:
#### Tags applied to every AWS resource in this stack.

---

Useful for cost tracking, access control, and organization. Stacktape automatically
adds `projectName`, `stage`, and `stackName` tags — your custom tags are merged on top.

Max 45 tags.

_MdxDesc.sd: Tags applied to every AWS resource in this stack.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Useful for cost tracking, access control, and organization. Stacktape automatically adds projectName, stage, and stackName tags — your custom tags are merged on top.

Max 45 tags.

disableStackInfoSaving.description:
#### Stop saving stack info to a local file after each deployment.

---

By default, Stacktape saves resource details and custom outputs to
`.stacktape-stack-info/{stackName}.json` after every deploy.

_MdxDesc.sd: Stop saving stack info to a local file after each deployment.
_MdxDesc.ld: By default, Stacktape saves resource details and custom outputs to .stacktape-stack-info/{stackName}.json after every deploy.

stackInfoDirectory.description:
#### Directory for the stack info JSON file.

---

Relative to the project root.

_MdxDesc.sd: Directory for the stack info JSON file.
_MdxDesc.ld: Relative to the project root.

vpc.description: #### VPC configuration: reuse an existing VPC or configure NAT Gateways.

_MdxDesc.sd: VPC configuration: reuse an existing VPC or configure NAT Gateways.

name.description: #### Name of the output (used as the key in terminal and stack info file).

_MdxDesc.sd: Name of the output (used as the key in terminal and stack info file).

value.description: #### Value to output. Typically a directive like `$ResourceParam('myApi', 'url')`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Value to output. Typically a directive like $ResourceParam('myApi', 'url').

description.description: #### Human-readable description shown alongside the output.

_MdxDesc.sd: Human-readable description shown alongside the output.

export.description:
#### Make this output available to other CloudFormation stacks.

---

Exported outputs can be referenced from other stacks using `$CfStackOutput()`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Make this output available to other CloudFormation stacks.
_MdxDesc.ld: Exported outputs can be referenced from other stacks using $CfStackOutput().

_MdxDesc.sd: Tag name (1-128 characters).
Tag value (1-256 characters).

reuseVpc.description:
#### Share a VPC with another Stacktape stack or use an existing VPC.

---

Useful when this stack needs to access VPC-protected resources (databases, Redis)
from another stack. By default, each stack gets its own VPC.

> **Important:** Set this when first creating the stack. Adding it to an already
> deployed stack can cause resources to be replaced and **data to be lost**.

_MdxDesc.sd: Share a VPC with another Stacktape stack or use an existing VPC.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Useful when this stack needs to access VPC-protected resources (databases, Redis) from another stack. By default, each stack gets its own VPC.

Important: Set this when first creating the stack. Adding it to an already deployed stack can cause resources to be replaced and data to be lost.

nat.description:
#### NAT Gateway configuration for private subnets.

---

Only applies when you have workloads using `usePrivateSubnetsWithNAT: true`.
Controls how many availability zones get a NAT Gateway (affects cost and redundancy).

_MdxDesc.sd: NAT Gateway configuration for private subnets.
_MdxDesc.ld: Only applies when you have workloads using usePrivateSubnetsWithNAT: true. Controls how many availability zones get a NAT Gateway (affects cost and redundancy).

projectName.description:
#### Project name of the stack whose VPC you want to share.

---

Must be used together with `stage`. Cannot be combined with `vpcId`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Project name of the stack whose VPC you want to share.
_MdxDesc.ld: Must be used together with stage. Cannot be combined with vpcId.

stage.description:
#### Stage of the stack whose VPC you want to share.

---

Must be used together with `projectName`. Cannot be combined with `vpcId`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Stage of the stack whose VPC you want to share.
_MdxDesc.ld: Must be used together with projectName. Cannot be combined with vpcId.

vpcId.description:
#### Direct VPC ID to reuse (e.g., `vpc-1234567890abcdef0`).

---

Use this to connect to a VPC not managed by Stacktape. Cannot be combined
with `projectName`/`stage`.

The VPC must use a private CIDR range (10.x, 172.16-31.x, or 192.168.x)
and have at least 3 public subnets (subnets with a route to an Internet Gateway).

_MdxDesc.sd: Direct VPC ID to reuse (e.g., vpc-1234567890abcdef0).
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use this to connect to a VPC not managed by Stacktape. Cannot be combined with projectName/stage.

The VPC must use a private CIDR range (10.x, 172.16-31.x, or 192.168.x) and have at least 3 public subnets (subnets with a route to an Internet Gateway).

availabilityZones.description:
#### How many availability zones get a NAT Gateway (~$32/month each).

---

- **1**: Cheapest, but no redundancy if that AZ goes down.
- **2**: Balanced cost and availability.
- **3**: Highest availability.

Each NAT Gateway gets a static Elastic IP that persists across deployments —
useful for IP whitelisting with external services.

_MdxDesc.sd: How many availability zones get a NAT Gateway (~$32/month each).
_MdxDesc.ld:
• 1: Cheapest, but no redundancy if that AZ goes down.
  • 2: Balanced cost and availability.
  • 3: Highest availability.

Each NAT Gateway gets a static Elastic IP that persists across deployments — useful for IP whitelisting with external services.

ContainerWorkload.description:
#### Run multiple containers together as a single unit with shared compute resources.

---

For advanced setups: sidecars, init containers, or services that need multiple processes.
Supports Fargate (serverless) or EC2 (custom instances). Auto-scales horizontally.

overrides.description:
#### Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.sd: Run multiple containers together as a single unit with shared compute resources.
_MdxDesc.ld: For advanced setups: sidecars, init containers, or services that need multiple processes. Supports Fargate (serverless) or EC2 (custom instances). Auto-scales horizontally.

containers.description: #### Containers in this workload. They share compute resources and scale together.

_MdxDesc.sd: Containers in this workload. They share compute resources and scale together.

resources.description:
#### CPU, memory, and compute engine (Fargate or EC2).

---

- **Fargate** (set `cpu` + `memory`): Serverless, no servers to manage.
- **EC2** (set `instanceTypes`): Choose specific instance types for more control or GPU access.

_MdxDesc.sd: CPU, memory, and compute engine (Fargate or EC2).
_MdxDesc.ld:
• Fargate (set cpu + memory): Serverless, no servers to manage.
  • EC2 (set instanceTypes): Choose specific instance types for more control or GPU access.

scaling.description: #### Auto-scaling: how many instances and when to add/remove them.

_MdxDesc.sd: Auto-scaling: how many instances and when to add/remove them.

deployment.description: #### Gradual traffic shifting (canary/linear) for safe deployments. Requires an ALB integration.

_MdxDesc.sd: Gradual traffic shifting (canary/linear) for safe deployments. Requires an ALB integration.

enableRemoteSessions.description: #### Enable `stacktape container:session` for interactive shell access to running containers.

_MdxDesc.sd: Enable stacktape container:session for interactive shell access to running containers.

usePrivateSubnetsWithNAT.description:
#### Run in private subnets with a NAT Gateway for outbound internet. Gives you a static public IP.

---

Useful for IP whitelisting with third-party APIs. NAT Gateway costs ~$32/month per AZ + data processing fees.

_MdxDesc.sd: Run in private subnets with a NAT Gateway for outbound internet. Gives you a static public IP.
_MdxDesc.ld: Useful for IP whitelisting with third-party APIs. NAT Gateway costs ~$32/month per AZ + data processing fees.

connectTo.description:
#### Give this resource access to other resources in your stack.

---

List the names of resources this workload needs to communicate with. Stacktape automatically:
- **Grants IAM permissions** (e.g., S3 read/write, SQS send/receive)
- **Opens network access** (security group rules for databases, Redis)
- **Injects environment variables** with connection details: `STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[PARAM]`

Example: `connectTo: ["myDatabase", "myBucket"]` gives this workload full access to both
resources and injects `STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING`, `STP_MY_BUCKET_NAME`, etc.

---

#### What each resource type provides:

**`Bucket`** — read/write/delete objects → `NAME`, `ARN`

**`DynamoDbTable`** — CRUD + scan/query → `NAME`, `ARN`, `STREAM_ARN`

**`RelationalDatabase`** — network access + connection details → `CONNECTION_STRING`, `HOST`, `PORT`.
Aurora also gets `READER_CONNECTION_STRING`, `READER_HOST`.

**`MongoDbAtlasCluster`** — temporary credential-less access → `CONNECTION_STRING`

**`RedisCluster`** — network access → `HOST`, `READER_HOST`, `PORT`

**`Function`** — invoke permission → `ARN`

**`BatchJob`** — submit/list/terminate → `JOB_DEFINITION_ARN`, `STATE_MACHINE_ARN`

**`EventBus`** — publish events → `ARN`

**`UserAuthPool`** — full control → `ID`, `CLIENT_ID`, `ARN`

**`SqsQueue`** — send/receive/delete → `ARN`, `NAME`, `URL`

**`SnsTopic`** — publish/subscribe → `ARN`, `NAME`

**`UpstashRedis`** → `HOST`, `PORT`, `PASSWORD`, `REST_TOKEN`, `REST_URL`, `REDIS_URL`

**`PrivateService`** → `ADDRESS`

**`aws:ses`** — full SES email sending permissions

_MdxDesc.sd: Give this resource access to other resources in your stack.
_MdxDesc.ld:
List the names of resources this workload needs to communicate with. Stacktape automatically:

  • Grants IAM permissions (e.g., S3 read/write, SQS send/receive)
  • Opens network access (security group rules for databases, Redis)
  • Injects environment variables with connection details: STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[PARAM]

Example: connectTo: ["myDatabase", "myBucket"] gives this workload full access to both resources and injects STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING, STP_MY_BUCKET_NAME, etc.

iamRoleStatements.description:
#### Raw IAM policy statements for permissions not covered by `connectTo`.

---

Added as a separate policy alongside auto-generated permissions. Use this for
accessing AWS services directly (e.g., Rekognition, Textract, Bedrock).

_MdxDesc.sd: Raw IAM policy statements for permissions not covered by connectTo.
_MdxDesc.ld: Added as a separate policy alongside auto-generated permissions. Use this for accessing AWS services directly (e.g., Rekognition, Textract, Bedrock).

events.description: #### How this container receives traffic (API Gateway, load balancer, or service-connect).

_MdxDesc.sd: How this container receives traffic (API Gateway, load balancer, or service-connect).

loadBalancerHealthCheck.description: #### Load balancer health check settings. Only applies when integrated with an ALB or NLB.

_MdxDesc.sd: Load balancer health check settings. Only applies when integrated with an ALB or NLB.

name.description: #### Unique container name within this workload.

_MdxDesc.sd: Unique container name within this workload.

packaging.description: #### How to build or specify the container image.

_MdxDesc.sd: How to build or specify the container image.

essential.description: #### If `true` (default), the entire workload restarts when this container fails.

_MdxDesc.sd: If true (default), the entire workload restarts when this container fails.

logging.description: #### Container logging (stdout/stderr). Sent to CloudWatch, viewable with `stacktape logs`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Container logging (stdout/stderr). Sent to CloudWatch, viewable with stacktape logs.

dependsOn.description:
#### Start this container only after the listed containers reach a specific state.

---

E.g., wait for a database sidecar to be `HEALTHY` before starting the app container.

_MdxDesc.sd: Start this container only after the listed containers reach a specific state.
_MdxDesc.ld: E.g., wait for a database sidecar to be HEALTHY before starting the app container.

environment.description: #### Environment variables injected at runtime. Use `$ResourceParam()` or `$Secret()` for dynamic values.

_MdxDesc.sd: Environment variables injected at runtime. Use $ResourceParam() or $Secret() for dynamic values.

internalHealthCheck.description: #### Command-based health check. If it fails on an essential container, the workload instance is replaced.

_MdxDesc.sd: Command-based health check. If it fails on an essential container, the workload instance is replaced.

stopTimeout.description: #### Seconds to wait after SIGTERM before SIGKILL (2-120).

_MdxDesc.sd: Seconds to wait after SIGTERM before SIGKILL (2-120).

volumeMounts.description: #### Mount EFS volumes for persistent, shared storage across containers.

_MdxDesc.sd: Mount EFS volumes for persistent, shared storage across containers.

ContainerWorkloadLoadBalancerIntegration.description:
#### Triggers a container when a request matches the specified conditions on an Application Load Balancer.

---

You can route requests based on HTTP method, path, headers, query parameters, and source IP address.

_MdxDesc.sd: Triggers a container when a request matches the specified conditions on an Application Load Balancer.
_MdxDesc.ld: You can route requests based on HTTP method, path, headers, query parameters, and source IP address.

containerPort.description: #### The container port that will receive traffic from the load balancer.

_MdxDesc.sd: The container port that will receive traffic from the load balancer.

loadBalancerName.description:
#### The name of the Application Load Balancer.

---

This must reference a load balancer defined in your Stacktape configuration.

_MdxDesc.sd: The name of the Application Load Balancer.
_MdxDesc.ld: This must reference a load balancer defined in your Stacktape configuration.

listenerPort.description:
#### The port of the load balancer listener to attach to.

---

You only need to specify this if the load balancer uses custom listeners.

_MdxDesc.sd: The port of the load balancer listener to attach to.
_MdxDesc.ld: You only need to specify this if the load balancer uses custom listeners.

priority.description:
#### The priority of this integration rule.

---

Load balancer rules are evaluated in order from the lowest priority to the highest.
The first rule that matches an incoming request will handle it.

_MdxDesc.sd: The priority of this integration rule.
_MdxDesc.ld: Load balancer rules are evaluated in order from the lowest priority to the highest. The first rule that matches an incoming request will handle it.

paths.description:
#### A list of URL paths that will trigger this integration.

---

The request will be routed if its path matches any of the paths in this list.
The comparison is case-sensitive and supports `*` and `?` wildcards.

Example: `/users`, `/articles/*`

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of URL paths that will trigger this integration.
_MdxDesc.ld:
The request will be routed if its path matches any of the paths in this list. The comparison is case-sensitive and supports * and ? wildcards.

Example: /users, /articles/*

methods.description:
#### A list of HTTP methods that will trigger this integration.

---

Example: `GET`, `POST`, `DELETE`

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of HTTP methods that will trigger this integration.
_MdxDesc.ld: Example: GET, POST, DELETE

hosts.description:
#### A list of hostnames that will trigger this integration.

---

The hostname is parsed from the `Host` header of the request.
Wildcards (`*` and `?`) are supported.

Example: `api.example.com`, `*.myapp.com`

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of hostnames that will trigger this integration.
_MdxDesc.ld:
The hostname is parsed from the Host header of the request. Wildcards (* and ?) are supported.

Example: api.example.com, *.myapp.com

headers.description:
#### A list of header conditions that the request must match.

---

All header conditions must be met for the request to be routed.

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of header conditions that the request must match.
_MdxDesc.ld: All header conditions must be met for the request to be routed.

queryParams.description:
#### A list of query parameter conditions that the request must match.

---

All query parameter conditions must be met for the request to be routed.

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of query parameter conditions that the request must match.
_MdxDesc.ld: All query parameter conditions must be met for the request to be routed.

sourceIps.description:
#### A list of source IP addresses (in CIDR format) that are allowed to trigger this integration.

---

> **Note:** If the client is behind a proxy, this will be the IP address of the proxy.

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of source IP addresses (in CIDR format) that are allowed to trigger this integration.
_MdxDesc.ld: Note: If the client is behind a proxy, this will be the IP address of the proxy.

headerName.description: #### The name of the HTTP header.

_MdxDesc.sd: The name of the HTTP header.

values.description:
#### A list of allowed values for the header.

---

The condition is met if the header's value in the incoming request matches any of the values in this list. The comparison is case-insensitive.

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of allowed values for the header.
_MdxDesc.ld: The condition is met if the header's value in the incoming request matches any of the values in this list. The comparison is case-insensitive.

paramName.description: #### The name of the query parameter.

_MdxDesc.sd: The name of the query parameter.

values.description:
#### A list of allowed values for the query parameter.

---

The condition is met if the query parameter's value in the incoming request matches any of the values in this list. The comparison is case-insensitive.

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of allowed values for the query parameter.
_MdxDesc.ld: The condition is met if the query parameter's value in the incoming request matches any of the values in this list. The comparison is case-insensitive.

ContainerWorkloadHttpApiIntegration.description:
#### Triggers a container when an HTTP API Gateway receives a matching request.

---

You can route requests based on HTTP method and path.

_MdxDesc.sd: Triggers a container when an HTTP API Gateway receives a matching request.
_MdxDesc.ld: You can route requests based on HTTP method and path.

containerPort.description: #### The container port that will receive traffic from the API Gateway.

_MdxDesc.sd: The container port that will receive traffic from the API Gateway.

httpApiGatewayName.description: #### The name of the HTTP API Gateway.

_MdxDesc.sd: The name of the HTTP API Gateway.

method.description:
#### The HTTP method that will trigger this integration.

---

You can specify an exact method (e.g., `GET`) or use `*` to match any method.

_MdxDesc.sd: The HTTP method that will trigger this integration.
_MdxDesc.ld: You can specify an exact method (e.g., GET) or use * to match any method.

path.description:
#### The URL path that will trigger this integration.

---

- **Exact path**: `/users`
- **Path with parameter**: `/users/{id}`. The `id` will be available in `event.pathParameters.id`.
- **Greedy path**: `/files/{proxy+}`. This will match any path starting with `/files/`.

_MdxDesc.sd: The URL path that will trigger this integration.
_MdxDesc.ld:
• Exact path: /users
  • Path with parameter: /users/{id}. The id will be available in event.pathParameters.id.
  • Greedy path: /files/{proxy+}. This will match any path starting with /files/.

authorizer.description:
#### An authorizer to protect this route.

---

Unauthorized requests will be rejected with a `401 Unauthorized` response.

_MdxDesc.sd: An authorizer to protect this route.
_MdxDesc.ld: Unauthorized requests will be rejected with a 401 Unauthorized response.

payloadFormat.description:
#### The payload format version for the Lambda integration.

---

For details on the differences between formats, see the [AWS documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/latest/developerguide/http-api-develop-integrations-lambda.html).

_MdxDesc.sd: The payload format version for the Lambda integration.
_MdxDesc.ld: For details on the differences between formats, see the AWS documentation.

type.description:
#### Cognito JWT authorizer

---

Configures an HTTP API authorizer that validates JSON Web Tokens (JWTs) issued by a Cognito user pool.
This is the simplest way to protect routes when your users sign in via `user-auth-pool`.

Stacktape turns this into an API Gateway v2 authorizer of type `JWT` that checks the token's issuer and audience.

_MdxDesc.sd: Cognito JWT authorizer
_MdxDesc.ld:
Configures an HTTP API authorizer that validates JSON Web Tokens (JWTs) issued by a Cognito user pool. This is the simplest way to protect routes when your users sign in via user-auth-pool.

Stacktape turns this into an API Gateway v2 authorizer of type JWT that checks the token's issuer and audience.

userPoolName.description:
#### Name of the user pool to protect the API

---

The Stacktape name of the `user-auth-pool` resource whose tokens should be accepted by this HTTP API authorizer.
Stacktape uses this to:

- Set the expected **audience** to the user pool client ID.
- Build the expected **issuer** URL based on the user pool and AWS region.

In practice this means only JWTs issued by this pool (and its client) will be considered valid.

_MdxDesc.sd: Name of the user pool to protect the API
_MdxDesc.ld:
The Stacktape name of the user-auth-pool resource whose tokens should be accepted by this HTTP API authorizer. Stacktape uses this to:

  • Set the expected audience to the user pool client ID.
  • Build the expected issuer URL based on the user pool and AWS region.

In practice this means only JWTs issued by this pool (and its client) will be considered valid.

identitySources.description:
#### Where to read the JWT from in the request

---

A list of identity sources that tell API Gateway where to look for the bearer token, using the
`$request.*` syntax from API Gateway (for example `'$request.header.Authorization'`).

If you omit this, Stacktape defaults to reading the token from the `Authorization` HTTP header,
using a JWT authorizer as described in the API Gateway v2 authorizer docs
([AWS::ApiGatewayV2::Authorizer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/TemplateReference/aws-resource-apigatewayv2-authorizer)).

_MdxDesc.sd: Where to read the JWT from in the request
_MdxDesc.ld:
A list of identity sources that tell API Gateway where to look for the bearer token, using the $request.* syntax from API Gateway (for example '$request.header.Authorization').

If you omit this, Stacktape defaults to reading the token from the Authorization HTTP header, using a JWT authorizer as described in the API Gateway v2 authorizer docs (AWS::ApiGatewayV2::Authorizer).

type.description:
#### Lambda-based HTTP API authorizer

---

Configures an API Gateway **request** authorizer that runs a Lambda function to decide whether a request is allowed.
This is useful when your authorization logic can't be expressed as simple JWT validation – for example when you
check API keys, look up permissions in a database, or integrate with a non-JWT identity system.

Stacktape creates an `AWS::ApiGatewayV2::Authorizer` of type `REQUEST` and wires it up to your Lambda.

_MdxDesc.sd: Lambda-based HTTP API authorizer
_MdxDesc.ld:
Configures an API Gateway request authorizer that runs a Lambda function to decide whether a request is allowed. This is useful when your authorization logic can't be expressed as simple JWT validation – for example when you check API keys, look up permissions in a database, or integrate with a non-JWT identity system.

Stacktape creates an AWS::ApiGatewayV2::Authorizer of type REQUEST and wires it up to your Lambda.

functionName.description:
#### Name of the authorizer function

---

The Stacktape name of a `function` resource that should run for each authorized request.
API Gateway calls this Lambda, passes request details, and uses its response to allow or deny access.

_MdxDesc.sd: Name of the authorizer function
_MdxDesc.ld: The Stacktape name of a function resource that should run for each authorized request. API Gateway calls this Lambda, passes request details, and uses its response to allow or deny access.

iamResponse.description:
#### Use IAM-style (v1) authorizer responses

---

- If `true`, your Lambda must return a full IAM policy document (the "v1" format).
- If `false` or omitted, Stacktape enables **simple responses** (the HTTP API v2 payload format)
  so your Lambda can return a small JSON object with an `isAuthorized` flag and optional context.

This flag is wired to `EnableSimpleResponses` on the underlying `AWS::ApiGatewayV2::Authorizer`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Use IAM-style (v1) authorizer responses
_MdxDesc.ld:
• If true, your Lambda must return a full IAM policy document (the "v1" format).
  • If false or omitted, Stacktape enables simple responses (the HTTP API v2 payload format) so your Lambda can return a small JSON object with an isAuthorized flag and optional context.

This flag is wired to EnableSimpleResponses on the underlying AWS::ApiGatewayV2::Authorizer.

identitySources.description:
#### Where to read identity data from

---

A list of request fields API Gateway should pass into your Lambda authorizer (for example headers, query parameters,
or stage variables) using the `$request.*` syntax.

When left empty, no specific identity sources are configured and your Lambda must inspect the incoming event directly.

_MdxDesc.sd: Where to read identity data from
_MdxDesc.ld:
A list of request fields API Gateway should pass into your Lambda authorizer (for example headers, query parameters, or stage variables) using the $request.* syntax.

When left empty, no specific identity sources are configured and your Lambda must inspect the incoming event directly.

cacheResultSeconds.description:
#### Cache authorizer results

---

Number of seconds API Gateway should cache the result of the Lambda authorizer for a given identity.
While cached, repeated requests skip calling your authorizer function and reuse the previous result.

This value is applied to `AuthorizerResultTtlInSeconds`. If omitted, Stacktape sets it to `0` (no caching).

_MdxDesc.sd: Cache authorizer results
_MdxDesc.ld:
Number of seconds API Gateway should cache the result of the Lambda authorizer for a given identity. While cached, repeated requests skip calling your authorizer function and reuse the previous result.

This value is applied to AuthorizerResultTtlInSeconds. If omitted, Stacktape sets it to 0 (no caching).

ContainerWorkloadInternalIntegration.description: #### Opens a container port for connections from other containers within the same workload.

_MdxDesc.sd: Opens a container port for connections from other containers within the same workload.

containerPort.description: #### The container port to open for internal traffic.

_MdxDesc.sd: The container port to open for internal traffic.

ContainerWorkloadServiceConnectIntegrati.description: #### Opens a container port for connections from other compute resources in the same stack.

_MdxDesc.sd: Opens a container port for connections from other compute resources in the same stack.

containerPort.description: #### The container port to open for service-to-service communication.

_MdxDesc.sd: The container port to open for service-to-service communication.

alias.description:
#### An alias for this service, used for service discovery.

---

Other resources in the stack can connect to this service using a URL like `protocol://alias:port` (e.g., `http://my-service:8080`).
By default, the alias is derived from the resource and container names (e.g., `my-resource-my-container`).

_MdxDesc.sd: An alias for this service, used for service discovery.
_MdxDesc.ld: Other resources in the stack can connect to this service using a URL like protocol://alias:port (e.g., http://my-service:8080). By default, the alias is derived from the resource and container names (e.g., my-resource-my-container).

protocol.description:
#### The protocol used for service-to-service communication.

---

Specifying the protocol allows AWS to capture protocol-specific metrics, such as the number of HTTP 5xx errors.

_MdxDesc.sd: The protocol used for service-to-service communication.
_MdxDesc.ld: Specifying the protocol allows AWS to capture protocol-specific metrics, such as the number of HTTP 5xx errors.

ContainerWorkloadNetworkLoadBalancerInte.description:
#### Triggers a container when a request is made to a Network Load Balancer.

---

A Network Load Balancer operates at the transport layer (Layer 4) and can handle TCP and TLS traffic.

_MdxDesc.sd: Triggers a container when a request is made to a Network Load Balancer.
_MdxDesc.ld: A Network Load Balancer operates at the transport layer (Layer 4) and can handle TCP and TLS traffic.

containerPort.description: #### The container port that will receive traffic from the load balancer.

_MdxDesc.sd: The container port that will receive traffic from the load balancer.

loadBalancerName.description:
#### The name of the Network Load Balancer.

---

This must reference a load balancer defined in your Stacktape configuration.

_MdxDesc.sd: The name of the Network Load Balancer.
_MdxDesc.ld: This must reference a load balancer defined in your Stacktape configuration.

listenerPort.description: #### The port of the listener that will forward traffic to this integration.

_MdxDesc.sd: The port of the listener that will forward traffic to this integration.

healthcheckPath.description: #### Path the load balancer pings to check container health.

_MdxDesc.sd: Path the load balancer pings to check container health.
Seconds between health checks.

healthcheckTimeout.description: #### Seconds before a health check is considered failed.

_MdxDesc.sd: Seconds before a health check is considered failed.

healthCheckProtocol.description: #### Health check protocol. ALB defaults to `HTTP`, NLB defaults to `TCP`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Health check protocol. ALB defaults to HTTP, NLB defaults to TCP.

healthCheckPort.description: #### Health check port. Defaults to the traffic port.

_MdxDesc.sd: Health check port. Defaults to the traffic port.

StpBuildpackCwImagePackaging.description:
#### A zero-config buildpack that creates a container image from your source code.

---

The `stacktape-image-buildpack` automatically bundles your code and dependencies into an optimized container image.

**Supported languages:** JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, and Go.

For JS/TS, your code is bundled into a single file with source maps.
The resulting image is uploaded to a managed ECR repository.

_MdxDesc.sd: A zero-config buildpack that creates a container image from your source code.
_MdxDesc.ld:
The stacktape-image-buildpack automatically bundles your code and dependencies into an optimized container image.

Supported languages: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, and Go.

For JS/TS, your code is bundled into a single file with source maps. The resulting image is uploaded to a managed ECR repository.

StpBuildpackCwImagePackagingProps.description: #### Configures an image to be built automatically by Stacktape from your source code.

_MdxDesc.sd: Language-specific packaging configuration.

requiresGlibcBinaries.description:
#### Use glibc instead of musl (Alpine default). Enable if native dependencies require glibc.

---

Results in a larger image. Common packages needing this: `sharp`, `canvas`, `bcrypt`, `puppeteer`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Use glibc instead of musl (Alpine default). Enable if native dependencies require glibc.
_MdxDesc.ld: Results in a larger image. Common packages needing this: sharp, canvas, bcrypt, puppeteer.

customDockerBuildCommands.description:
#### A list of commands to be executed during the `docker build` process.

---

These commands are executed using the `RUN` directive in the Dockerfile.
This is useful for installing additional system dependencies in your container.

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of commands to be executed during the docker build process.
_MdxDesc.ld: These commands are executed using the RUN directive in the Dockerfile. This is useful for installing additional system dependencies in your container.

entryfilePath.description:
#### Path to your app's entry point, relative to the Stacktape config file.

---

For JS/TS: code is bundled into a single file. Dependencies with native binaries are installed separately.
For Python: use `module/file.py:app` format when using `runAppAs` (WSGI/ASGI).

_MdxDesc.sd: Path to your app's entry point, relative to the Stacktape config file.
_MdxDesc.ld: For JS/TS: code is bundled into a single file. Dependencies with native binaries are installed separately. For Python: use module/file.py:app format when using runAppAs (WSGI/ASGI).

includeFiles.description:
#### A glob pattern of files to explicitly include in the deployment package.

---

The path is relative to your Stacktape configuration file.

_MdxDesc.sd: A glob pattern of files to explicitly include in the deployment package.
_MdxDesc.ld: The path is relative to your Stacktape configuration file.

excludeFiles.description:
#### A glob pattern of files to explicitly exclude from the deployment package.

---

_MdxDesc.sd: A glob pattern of files to explicitly exclude from the deployment package.

excludeDependencies.description: #### A list of dependencies to exclude from the deployment package.

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of dependencies to exclude from the deployment package.
Configures an image to be built automatically by Stacktape from your source code.

tsConfigPath.description:
#### The path to the `tsconfig.json` file.

---

This is primarily used to resolve path aliases during the build process.

_MdxDesc.sd: The path to the tsconfig.json file.
_MdxDesc.ld: This is primarily used to resolve path aliases during the build process.

emitTsDecoratorMetadata.description: #### Emit TypeScript decorator metadata. Required by NestJS, TypeORM, and similar frameworks.

_MdxDesc.sd: Emit TypeScript decorator metadata. Required by NestJS, TypeORM, and similar frameworks.

dependenciesToExcludeFromBundle.description:
#### A list of dependencies to exclude from the main bundle.

---

These dependencies will be treated as "external" and will not be bundled directly into your application's code.
Instead, they will be installed separately in the deployment package.
Use `*` to exclude all dependencies from the bundle.

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of dependencies to exclude from the main bundle.
_MdxDesc.ld: These dependencies will be treated as "external" and will not be bundled directly into your application's code. Instead, they will be installed separately in the deployment package. Use * to exclude all dependencies from the bundle.

outputModuleFormat.description:
#### Output module format: `cjs` (CommonJS) or `esm` (ES Modules, enables top-level `await`).

---

**Note:** Some npm packages don't support ESM. ESM may also produce less readable stack traces.

_MdxDesc.sd: Output module format: cjs (CommonJS) or esm (ES Modules, enables top-level await).
_MdxDesc.ld: Note: Some npm packages don't support ESM. ESM may also produce less readable stack traces.

nodeVersion.description: #### The major version of Node.js to use.

_MdxDesc.sd: The major version of Node.js to use.

disableSourceMaps.description: #### Skip generating source maps. Reduces package size but makes production errors harder to debug.

_MdxDesc.sd: Skip generating source maps. Reduces package size but makes production errors harder to debug.

outputSourceMapsTo.description:
#### Save source maps to a local directory instead of uploading them to AWS.

---

Useful for uploading to external error tracking (Sentry, Datadog, etc.). CloudWatch stack traces won't be mapped.

_MdxDesc.sd: Save source maps to a local directory instead of uploading them to AWS.
_MdxDesc.ld: Useful for uploading to external error tracking (Sentry, Datadog, etc.). CloudWatch stack traces won't be mapped.

dependenciesToExcludeFromDeploymentPacka.description:
#### A list of dependencies to exclude from the deployment package.

---

This only applies to dependencies that are not statically bundled.
To exclude a dependency from the static bundle, use `dependenciesToExcludeFromBundle`.
Use `*` to exclude all non-bundled dependencies.

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of dependencies to exclude from the deployment package.
_MdxDesc.ld: This only applies to dependencies that are not statically bundled. To exclude a dependency from the static bundle, use dependenciesToExcludeFromBundle. Use * to exclude all non-bundled dependencies.

packageManagerFile.description:
#### The path to your project's dependency file.

---

This can be a `requirements.txt`, `Pipfile`, or `pyproject.toml` file.

_MdxDesc.sd: The path to your project's dependency file.
_MdxDesc.ld: This can be a requirements.txt, Pipfile, or pyproject.toml file.

packageManager.description:
#### The Python package manager to use.

---

Stacktape uses `uv` for dependency resolution and installation. This option is kept
for compatibility and must be set to `uv` if provided.

_MdxDesc.sd: The Python package manager to use.
_MdxDesc.ld: Stacktape uses uv for dependency resolution and installation. This option is kept for compatibility and must be set to uv if provided.

pythonVersion.description: #### The version of Python to use.

_MdxDesc.sd: The version of Python to use.

runAppAs.description:
#### Python server type: `WSGI` (Flask, Django) or `ASGI` (FastAPI, Starlette).

---

Only for `stacktape-image-buildpack`. Auto-binds to the `PORT` env var.
Set `entryfilePath` to `module/file.py:app` (e.g., `app/main.py:app`).

_MdxDesc.sd: Python server type: WSGI (Flask, Django) or ASGI (FastAPI, Starlette).
_MdxDesc.ld: Only for stacktape-image-buildpack. Auto-binds to the PORT env var. Set entryfilePath to module/file.py:app (e.g., app/main.py:app).

minify.description: #### Minify Python code to reduce package size. Makes production stack traces harder to read.

_MdxDesc.sd: Minify Python code to reduce package size. Makes production stack traces harder to read.

useMaven.description:
#### Specifies whether to use Maven instead of Gradle.

---

By default, Stacktape uses Gradle to build Java projects.

_MdxDesc.sd: Specifies whether to use Maven instead of Gradle.
_MdxDesc.ld: By default, Stacktape uses Gradle to build Java projects.

packageManagerFile.description: #### The path to your project's build file (`pom.xml` for Maven or `build.gradle` for Gradle).

_MdxDesc.sd: The path to your project's build file (pom.xml for Maven or build.gradle for Gradle).

javaVersion.description: #### The version of Java to use.

_MdxDesc.sd: The version of Java to use.

phpVersion.description: #### The version of PHP to use.

_MdxDesc.sd: The version of PHP to use.

projectFile.description: #### The path to your .NET project file (.csproj).

_MdxDesc.sd: The path to your .NET project file (.csproj).
The version of .NET to use.

rubyVersion.description: #### The version of Ruby to use.

_MdxDesc.sd: The version of Ruby to use.

ExternalBuildpackCwImagePackaging.description:
#### Builds a container image using an external buildpack.

---

External buildpacks (buildpacks.io) automatically detect your application type
and build an optimized container image with zero configuration.

The default builder is `paketobuildpacks/builder-jammy-base`.
You can find buildpacks for almost any language or framework.

_MdxDesc.sd: Builds a container image using an external buildpack.
_MdxDesc.ld:
External buildpacks (buildpacks.io) automatically detect your application type and build an optimized container image with zero configuration.

The default builder is paketobuildpacks/builder-jammy-base. You can find buildpacks for almost any language or framework.

_MdxDesc.sd: The Buildpack Builder to use.

buildpacks.description:
#### The specific Buildpack to use.

---

By default, the buildpack is detected automatically.

_MdxDesc.sd: The specific Buildpack to use.
_MdxDesc.ld: By default, the buildpack is detected automatically.

sourceDirectoryPath.description: #### The path to the source code directory.

_MdxDesc.sd: The path to the source code directory.

command.description:
#### A command to be executed when the container starts.

---

Example: `['/app/start.sh']`

_MdxDesc.sd: A command to be executed when the container starts.
_MdxDesc.ld: Example: ['/app/start.sh']

PrebuiltCwImagePackaging.description:
#### Uses a pre-built container image.

---

With `prebuilt-image`, you provide a reference to an existing container image.
This can be a public image from Docker Hub or a private image from any container registry.

For private registries, configure `repositoryCredentialsSecretArn` with credentials stored in AWS Secrets Manager.

_MdxDesc.sd: Uses a pre-built container image.
_MdxDesc.ld:
With prebuilt-image, you provide a reference to an existing container image. This can be a public image from Docker Hub or a private image from any container registry.

For private registries, configure repositoryCredentialsSecretArn with credentials stored in AWS Secrets Manager.

repositoryCredentialsSecretArn.description:
#### The ARN of a secret containing credentials for a private container registry.

---

The secret must be a JSON object with `username` and `password` keys.
You can create secrets using the `stacktape secret:create` command.

_MdxDesc.sd: The ARN of a secret containing credentials for a private container registry.
_MdxDesc.ld: The secret must be a JSON object with username and password keys. You can create secrets using the stacktape secret:create command.

entryPoint.description:
#### A script to be executed when the container starts.

---

This overrides the `ENTRYPOINT` instruction in the Dockerfile.

_MdxDesc.sd: A script to be executed when the container starts.
_MdxDesc.ld: This overrides the ENTRYPOINT instruction in the Dockerfile.

image.description: #### The name or URL of the container image.

_MdxDesc.sd: The name or URL of the container image.

command.description:
#### A command to be executed when the container starts.

---

This overrides the `CMD` instruction in the Dockerfile.

Example: `['/app/start.sh']`

_MdxDesc.sd: A command to be executed when the container starts.
_MdxDesc.ld:
This overrides the CMD instruction in the Dockerfile.

Example: ['/app/start.sh']

_MdxDesc.sd: Configures a pre-built container image.

CustomDockerfileCwImagePackaging.description:
#### Builds a container image from your own Dockerfile.

---

With `custom-dockerfile`, you provide a path to your Dockerfile and build context.
Stacktape builds the image and uploads it to a managed ECR repository.

This gives you full control over the container environment and is ideal for complex setups.

_MdxDesc.sd: Builds a container image from your own Dockerfile.
_MdxDesc.ld:
With custom-dockerfile, you provide a path to your Dockerfile and build context. Stacktape builds the image and uploads it to a managed ECR repository.

This gives you full control over the container environment and is ideal for complex setups.

- `HEALTHY`: The dependency has passed its first health check.

strategy.description:
#### How traffic shifts to the new version during deployment.

---

- `Canary10Percent5Minutes`: 10% first, then all after 5 min.
- `Canary10Percent15Minutes`: 10% first, then all after 15 min.
- `Linear10PercentEvery1Minutes`: 10% more every minute.
- `Linear10PercentEvery3Minutes`: 10% more every 3 minutes.
- `AllAtOnce`: Instant switch.

_MdxDesc.sd: How traffic shifts to the new version during deployment.
_MdxDesc.ld:
• Canary10Percent5Minutes: 10% first, then all after 5 min.
  • Canary10Percent15Minutes: 10% first, then all after 15 min.
  • Linear10PercentEvery1Minutes: 10% more every minute.
  • Linear10PercentEvery3Minutes: 10% more every 3 minutes.
  • AllAtOnce: Instant switch.

BatchJob.description:
#### Run containerized tasks to completion — data processing, ML training, video encoding, etc.

---

Pay only for the compute time used. Supports CPU and GPU workloads, retries on failure,
and can be triggered by schedules, HTTP requests, S3 uploads, or queue messages.

overrides.description:
#### Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.sd: Run containerized tasks to completion — data processing, ML training, video encoding, etc.
_MdxDesc.ld: Pay only for the compute time used. Supports CPU and GPU workloads, retries on failure, and can be triggered by schedules, HTTP requests, S3 uploads, or queue messages.

container.description: #### Docker container image and environment for the job.

_MdxDesc.sd: Docker container image and environment for the job.

resources.description: #### CPU, memory, and GPU requirements. AWS auto-provisions a matching instance.

_MdxDesc.sd: CPU, memory, and GPU requirements. AWS auto-provisions a matching instance.

timeout.description: #### Max run time in seconds. The job is killed if it exceeds this, then retried if `retryConfig` is set.

_MdxDesc.sd: Max run time in seconds. The job is killed if it exceeds this, then retried if retryConfig is set.

useSpotInstances.description:
#### Use discounted spare AWS capacity. Saves up to 90%, but jobs can be interrupted.

---

**Use this when:** Your job can safely be restarted from the beginning (e.g., data imports,
image processing, ML training with checkpoints). Combine with `retryConfig` to auto-retry
on interruption.

**Don't use when:** Your job has side effects that can't be repeated (e.g., sending emails,
charging payments) or must finish within a strict deadline.

If interrupted, your container gets a `SIGTERM` and 120 seconds to shut down gracefully.

_MdxDesc.sd: Use discounted spare AWS capacity. Saves up to 90%, but jobs can be interrupted.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use this when: Your job can safely be restarted from the beginning (e.g., data imports, image processing, ML training with checkpoints). Combine with retryConfig to auto-retry on interruption.

Don't use when: Your job has side effects that can't be repeated (e.g., sending emails, charging payments) or must finish within a strict deadline.

If interrupted, your container gets a SIGTERM and 120 seconds to shut down gracefully.

logging.description: #### Container logging (stdout/stderr). Sent to CloudWatch, viewable with `stacktape logs`.

retryConfig.description: #### Auto-retry on failure, timeout, or Spot interruption.

_MdxDesc.sd: Auto-retry on failure, timeout, or Spot interruption.

events.description: #### Events that trigger this job (schedules, HTTP requests, S3 uploads, SQS messages, etc.).

_MdxDesc.sd: Events that trigger this job (schedules, HTTP requests, S3 uploads, SQS messages, etc.).

connectTo.description:
#### Give this resource access to other resources in your stack.

---

List the names of resources this workload needs to communicate with. Stacktape automatically:
- **Grants IAM permissions** (e.g., S3 read/write, SQS send/receive)
- **Opens network access** (security group rules for databases, Redis)
- **Injects environment variables** with connection details: `STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[PARAM]`

Example: `connectTo: ["myDatabase", "myBucket"]` gives this workload full access to both
resources and injects `STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING`, `STP_MY_BUCKET_NAME`, etc.

---

#### What each resource type provides:

**`Bucket`** — read/write/delete objects → `NAME`, `ARN`

**`DynamoDbTable`** — CRUD + scan/query → `NAME`, `ARN`, `STREAM_ARN`

**`RelationalDatabase`** — network access + connection details → `CONNECTION_STRING`, `HOST`, `PORT`.
Aurora also gets `READER_CONNECTION_STRING`, `READER_HOST`.

**`MongoDbAtlasCluster`** — temporary credential-less access → `CONNECTION_STRING`

**`RedisCluster`** — network access → `HOST`, `READER_HOST`, `PORT`

**`Function`** — invoke permission → `ARN`

**`BatchJob`** — submit/list/terminate → `JOB_DEFINITION_ARN`, `STATE_MACHINE_ARN`

**`EventBus`** — publish events → `ARN`

**`UserAuthPool`** — full control → `ID`, `CLIENT_ID`, `ARN`

**`SqsQueue`** — send/receive/delete → `ARN`, `NAME`, `URL`

**`SnsTopic`** — publish/subscribe → `ARN`, `NAME`

**`UpstashRedis`** → `HOST`, `PORT`, `PASSWORD`, `REST_TOKEN`, `REST_URL`, `REDIS_URL`

**`PrivateService`** → `ADDRESS`

**`aws:ses`** — full SES email sending permissions

_MdxDesc.sd: Give this resource access to other resources in your stack.
_MdxDesc.ld:
List the names of resources this workload needs to communicate with. Stacktape automatically:

  • Grants IAM permissions (e.g., S3 read/write, SQS send/receive)
  • Opens network access (security group rules for databases, Redis)
  • Injects environment variables with connection details: STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[PARAM]

Example: connectTo: ["myDatabase", "myBucket"] gives this workload full access to both resources and injects STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING, STP_MY_BUCKET_NAME, etc.

_MdxDesc.ld: Added as a separate policy alongside auto-generated permissions. Use this for accessing AWS services directly (e.g., Rekognition, Textract, Bedrock).

#### Multiply wait time by this factor after each retry (exponential backoff).

---

E.g., with `retryIntervalSeconds: 5` and `retryIntervalMultiplier: 2`, waits are 5s, 10s, 20s, etc.

_MdxDesc.sd: Multiply wait time by this factor after each retry (exponential backoff).
_MdxDesc.ld: E.g., with retryIntervalSeconds: 5 and retryIntervalMultiplier: 2, waits are 5s, 10s, 20s, etc.

ApplicationLoadBalancerIntegration.description:
#### Triggers a function when an Application Load Balancer receives a matching HTTP request.

---

You can route requests based on HTTP method, path, headers, query parameters, and source IP address.

type.description:
#### Triggers a function when an Application Load Balancer receives a matching HTTP request.

---

You can route requests based on HTTP method, path, headers, query parameters, and source IP address.

_MdxDesc.sd: Triggers a function when an Application Load Balancer receives a matching HTTP request.
_MdxDesc.ld: You can route requests based on HTTP method, path, headers, query parameters, and source IP address.

_MdxDesc.sd: Triggers a function when an Application Load Balancer receives a matching HTTP request.
_MdxDesc.ld: You can route requests based on HTTP method, path, headers, query parameters, and source IP address.

loadBalancerName.description:
#### The name of the Application Load Balancer.

---

This must reference a load balancer defined in your Stacktape configuration.

_MdxDesc.sd: The name of the Application Load Balancer.
_MdxDesc.ld: This must reference a load balancer defined in your Stacktape configuration.

listenerPort.description:
#### The port of the load balancer listener to attach to.

---

You only need to specify this if the load balancer uses custom listeners.

_MdxDesc.sd: The port of the load balancer listener to attach to.
_MdxDesc.ld: You only need to specify this if the load balancer uses custom listeners.

priority.description:
#### The priority of this integration rule.

---

Load balancer rules are evaluated in order from the lowest priority to the highest.
The first rule that matches an incoming request will handle it.

_MdxDesc.sd: The priority of this integration rule.
_MdxDesc.ld: Load balancer rules are evaluated in order from the lowest priority to the highest. The first rule that matches an incoming request will handle it.

paths.description:
#### A list of URL paths that will trigger this integration.

---

The request will be routed if its path matches any of the paths in this list.
The comparison is case-sensitive and supports `*` and `?` wildcards.

Example: `/users`, `/articles/*`

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of URL paths that will trigger this integration.
_MdxDesc.ld:
The request will be routed if its path matches any of the paths in this list. The comparison is case-sensitive and supports * and ? wildcards.

Example: /users, /articles/*

methods.description:
#### A list of HTTP methods that will trigger this integration.

---

Example: `GET`, `POST`, `DELETE`

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of HTTP methods that will trigger this integration.
_MdxDesc.ld: Example: GET, POST, DELETE

hosts.description:
#### A list of hostnames that will trigger this integration.

---

The hostname is parsed from the `Host` header of the request.
Wildcards (`*` and `?`) are supported.

Example: `api.example.com`, `*.myapp.com`

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of hostnames that will trigger this integration.
_MdxDesc.ld:
The hostname is parsed from the Host header of the request. Wildcards (* and ?) are supported.

Example: api.example.com, *.myapp.com

headers.description:
#### A list of header conditions that the request must match.

---

All header conditions must be met for the request to be routed.

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of header conditions that the request must match.
_MdxDesc.ld: All header conditions must be met for the request to be routed.

queryParams.description:
#### A list of query parameter conditions that the request must match.

---

All query parameter conditions must be met for the request to be routed.

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of query parameter conditions that the request must match.
_MdxDesc.ld: All query parameter conditions must be met for the request to be routed.

sourceIps.description:
#### A list of source IP addresses (in CIDR format) that are allowed to trigger this integration.

---

> **Note:** If the client is behind a proxy, this will be the IP address of the proxy.

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of source IP addresses (in CIDR format) that are allowed to trigger this integration.
_MdxDesc.ld: Note: If the client is behind a proxy, this will be the IP address of the proxy.

SnsIntegration.description:
#### Triggers a function when a new message is published to an SNS topic.

---

SNS is a pub/sub messaging service. Reference a topic from your stack's `snsTopics` or use an external ARN.

_MdxDesc.sd: Triggers a function when a new message is published to an SNS topic.
_MdxDesc.ld: SNS is a pub/sub messaging service. Reference a topic from your stack's snsTopics or use an external ARN.

snsTopicName.description:
#### The name of an SNS topic defined in your stack's resources.

---

You must specify either `snsTopicName` or `snsTopicArn`.

_MdxDesc.sd: The name of an SNS topic defined in your stack's resources.
_MdxDesc.ld: You must specify either snsTopicName or snsTopicArn.

snsTopicArn.description:
#### The ARN of an existing SNS topic.

---

Use this to subscribe to a topic that is not managed by your stack.
You must specify either `snsTopicName` or `snsTopicArn`.

_MdxDesc.sd: The ARN of an existing SNS topic.
_MdxDesc.ld: Use this to subscribe to a topic that is not managed by your stack. You must specify either snsTopicName or snsTopicArn.

filterPolicy.description:
#### Filter messages by attributes so only relevant ones trigger the function.

---

Uses SNS subscription filter policy syntax. For content-based filtering, use EventBridge instead.

onDeliveryFailure.description:
#### A destination for messages that fail to be delivered to the target.

---

In rare cases (e.g., if the target function cannot scale fast enough), a message might fail to be delivered.
This property specifies an SQS queue where failed messages will be sent.

_MdxDesc.sd: A destination for messages that fail to be delivered to the target.
_MdxDesc.ld: In rare cases (e.g., if the target function cannot scale fast enough), a message might fail to be delivered. This property specifies an SQS queue where failed messages will be sent.

sqsQueueArn.description: #### The ARN of the SQS queue for failed messages.

_MdxDesc.sd: The ARN of the SQS queue for failed messages.

sqsQueueName.description: #### The name of an SQS queue (defined in your Stacktape configuration) for failed messages.

_MdxDesc.sd: The name of an SQS queue (defined in your Stacktape configuration) for failed messages.

SqsIntegration.description:
#### Triggers a function when new messages are available in an SQS queue.

---

Messages are processed in batches. The function fires when `batchSize` is reached,
`maxBatchWindowSeconds` expires, or the 6 MB payload limit is hit.

**Important:** A single SQS queue should only have one consumer function. For fan-out (multiple
consumers for the same message), use an SNS topic or EventBridge event bus instead.

_MdxDesc.sd: Triggers a function when new messages are available in an SQS queue.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Messages are processed in batches. The function fires when batchSize is reached, maxBatchWindowSeconds expires, or the 6 MB payload limit is hit.

Important: A single SQS queue should only have one consumer function. For fan-out (multiple consumers for the same message), use an SNS topic or EventBridge event bus instead.

sqsQueueName.description:
#### The name of an SQS queue defined in your stack's resources.

---

You must specify either `sqsQueueName` or `sqsQueueArn`.

_MdxDesc.sd: The name of an SQS queue defined in your stack's resources.
_MdxDesc.ld: You must specify either sqsQueueName or sqsQueueArn.

sqsQueueArn.description:
#### The ARN of an existing SQS queue.

---

Use this to consume messages from a queue that is not managed by your stack.
You must specify either `sqsQueueName` or `sqsQueueArn`.

_MdxDesc.sd: The ARN of an existing SQS queue.
_MdxDesc.ld: Use this to consume messages from a queue that is not managed by your stack. You must specify either sqsQueueName or sqsQueueArn.

batchSize.description:
#### The maximum number of records to process in a single batch.

---

Maximum is 10,000.

_MdxDesc.sd: The maximum number of records to process in a single batch.
_MdxDesc.ld: Maximum is 10,000.

maxBatchWindowSeconds.description:
#### The maximum time (in seconds) to wait before invoking the function with a batch of records.

---

Maximum is 300 seconds. If not set, the function is invoked as soon as messages are available.

_MdxDesc.sd: The maximum time (in seconds) to wait before invoking the function with a batch of records.
_MdxDesc.ld: Maximum is 300 seconds. If not set, the function is invoked as soon as messages are available.

KinesisIntegration.description:
#### Triggers a function when new records are available in a Kinesis Data Stream.

---

Records are processed in batches. Two consumption modes:
- **Direct**: Polls each shard ~1/sec, throughput shared with other consumers.
- **Stream Consumer** (`autoCreateConsumer`): Dedicated connection per shard — higher throughput, lower latency.

_MdxDesc.sd: Triggers a function when new records are available in a Kinesis Data Stream.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Records are processed in batches. Two consumption modes:

  • Direct: Polls each shard ~1/sec, throughput shared with other consumers.
  • Stream Consumer (autoCreateConsumer): Dedicated connection per shard — higher throughput, lower latency.

kinesisStreamName.description:
#### The name of a Kinesis stream defined in your stack's resources.

---

You must specify either `kinesisStreamName` or `streamArn`.

_MdxDesc.sd: The name of a Kinesis stream defined in your stack's resources.
_MdxDesc.ld: You must specify either kinesisStreamName or streamArn.

streamArn.description:
#### The ARN of an existing Kinesis stream to consume records from.

---

Use this to consume from a stream that is not managed by your stack.
You must specify either `kinesisStreamName` or `streamArn`.

_MdxDesc.sd: The ARN of an existing Kinesis stream to consume records from.
_MdxDesc.ld: Use this to consume from a stream that is not managed by your stack. You must specify either kinesisStreamName or streamArn.

consumerArn.description:
#### The ARN of a specific stream consumer to use.

---

This cannot be used with `autoCreateConsumer`.

_MdxDesc.sd: The ARN of a specific stream consumer to use.
_MdxDesc.ld: This cannot be used with autoCreateConsumer.

autoCreateConsumer.description:
#### Automatically creates a dedicated stream consumer for this integration.

---

This is recommended for minimizing latency and maximizing throughput.
For more details, see the [AWS documentation on stream consumers](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/streams/latest/dev/amazon-kinesis-consumers.html).
This cannot be used with `consumerArn`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Automatically creates a dedicated stream consumer for this integration.
_MdxDesc.ld: This is recommended for minimizing latency and maximizing throughput. For more details, see the AWS documentation on stream consumers. This cannot be used with consumerArn.

maxBatchWindowSeconds.description:
#### The maximum time (in seconds) to wait before invoking the function with a batch of records.

---

Maximum is 300 seconds.

_MdxDesc.sd: The maximum time (in seconds) to wait before invoking the function with a batch of records.
_MdxDesc.ld: Maximum is 300 seconds.

batchSize.description:
#### The maximum number of records to process in a single batch.

---

Maximum is 10,000.

_MdxDesc.sd: The maximum number of records to process in a single batch.
_MdxDesc.ld: Maximum is 10,000.

startingPosition.description:
#### The position in the stream from which to start reading records.

---

- `LATEST`: Read only new records.
- `TRIM_HORIZON`: Read all available records from the beginning of the stream.

_MdxDesc.sd: The position in the stream from which to start reading records.
_MdxDesc.ld:
• LATEST: Read only new records.
  • TRIM_HORIZON: Read all available records from the beginning of the stream.

maximumRetryAttempts.description:
#### The number of times to retry a failed batch of records.

---

> **Important:** If an error occurs, the entire batch is retried, including records that were processed successfully. Your function should be idempotent to handle this.

_MdxDesc.sd: The number of times to retry a failed batch of records.
_MdxDesc.ld: Important: If an error occurs, the entire batch is retried, including records that were processed successfully. Your function should be idempotent to handle this.

onFailure.description: #### A destination (SQS queue or SNS topic) for batches that fail after all retry attempts.

_MdxDesc.sd: A destination (SQS queue or SNS topic) for batches that fail after all retry attempts.

parallelizationFactor.description: #### The number of batches to process concurrently from the same shard.

_MdxDesc.sd: The number of batches to process concurrently from the same shard.

bisectBatchOnFunctionError.description:
#### Splits a failed batch in two before retrying.

---

This can be useful if a failure is caused by a batch being too large.

_MdxDesc.sd: Splits a failed batch in two before retrying.
_MdxDesc.ld: This can be useful if a failure is caused by a batch being too large.

arn.description: #### The ARN of the SNS topic or SQS queue for failed batches.

_MdxDesc.sd: The ARN of the SNS topic or SQS queue for failed batches.
The type of the destination.
