DevModeConfig.description:
#### Dev Mode Configuration

---

Controls whether this resource runs locally or connects to the deployed AWS version
during `stacktape dev`.

remote.description:
#### Use the deployed AWS resource instead of a local emulation.

---

By default, databases, Redis, and DynamoDB run locally in Docker during dev mode.
Set to `true` to connect to the real deployed resource instead (must be deployed first).

Useful when local emulation doesn't match production behavior closely enough,
or when you need to work with real data.

_MdxDesc.sd: Use the deployed AWS resource instead of a local emulation.
_MdxDesc.ld:
By default, databases, Redis, and DynamoDB run locally in Docker during dev mode. Set to true to connect to the real deployed resource instead (must be deployed first).

Useful when local emulation doesn't match production behavior closely enough, or when you need to work with real data.

_MdxDesc.sd: Dev Mode Configuration
_MdxDesc.ld: Controls whether this resource runs locally or connects to the deployed AWS version during stacktape dev.

ApplicationLoadBalancer.description:
#### HTTP/HTTPS load balancer with flat ~$18/month pricing. Required for WebSockets, firewalls, and gradual deployments.

---

Routes requests to containers or Lambda functions based on path, host, headers, or query params.
More cost-effective than API Gateway above ~500k requests/day. AWS Free Tier eligible.

overrides.description:
#### Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.sd: HTTP/HTTPS load balancer with flat ~$18/month pricing. Required for WebSockets, firewalls, and gradual deployments.
_MdxDesc.ld: Routes requests to containers or Lambda functions based on path, host, headers, or query params. More cost-effective than API Gateway above ~500k requests/day. AWS Free Tier eligible.

interface.description: #### `internet` (public) or `internal` (VPC-only). Internal ALBs are not reachable from the internet.

_MdxDesc.sd: internet (public) or internal (VPC-only). Internal ALBs are not reachable from the internet.

customDomains.description:
#### Custom domains.

---

By default, Stacktape creates DNS records and TLS certificates for each domain.
If you manage DNS yourself, set `disableDnsRecordCreation` and provide `customCertificateArn`.

Backward compatible format `string[]` is still supported.

_MdxDesc.sd: Custom domains.
_MdxDesc.ld:
By default, Stacktape creates DNS records and TLS certificates for each domain. If you manage DNS yourself, set disableDnsRecordCreation and provide customCertificateArn.

Backward compatible format string[] is still supported.

listeners.description: #### Custom listeners (port + protocol). Defaults to HTTPS on 443 + HTTP on 80 (redirecting to HTTPS).

_MdxDesc.sd: Custom listeners (port + protocol). Defaults to HTTPS on 443 + HTTP on 80 (redirecting to HTTPS).

cdn.description: #### Put a CDN (CloudFront) in front of this load balancer for caching and lower latency worldwide.

_MdxDesc.sd: Put a CDN (CloudFront) in front of this load balancer for caching and lower latency worldwide.

alarms.description: #### Alarms for this load balancer (merged with global alarms from the Stacktape Console).

_MdxDesc.sd: Alarms for this load balancer (merged with global alarms from the Stacktape Console).

disabledGlobalAlarms.description: #### Global alarm names to exclude from this load balancer.

_MdxDesc.sd: Global alarm names to exclude from this load balancer.

useFirewall.description: #### Name of a `web-app-firewall` resource to protect this load balancer from common web exploits.

_MdxDesc.sd: Name of a web-app-firewall resource to protect this load balancer from common web exploits.

protocol.description: #### Listener protocol. `HTTPS` requires a TLS certificate (auto-created with `customDomains` or via `customCertificateArns`).

_MdxDesc.sd: Listener protocol. HTTPS requires a TLS certificate (auto-created with customDomains or via customCertificateArns).

port.description: #### Port this listener accepts traffic on (e.g., 443 for HTTPS, 80 for HTTP).

_MdxDesc.sd: Port this listener accepts traffic on (e.g., 443 for HTTPS, 80 for HTTP).

customCertificateArns.description: #### ARNs of your own ACM certificates. Not needed if using `customDomains` (Stacktape creates certs automatically).

_MdxDesc.sd: ARNs of your own ACM certificates. Not needed if using customDomains (Stacktape creates certs automatically).

whitelistIps.description: #### Restrict access to specific IP addresses/CIDRs. Default: all IPs allowed.

_MdxDesc.sd: Restrict access to specific IP addresses/CIDRs. Default: all IPs allowed.

defaultAction.description: #### Action for requests that don't match any integration. Currently supports `redirect` only.

_MdxDesc.sd: Action for requests that don't match any integration. Currently supports redirect only.

type.description: #### The type of the default action.

_MdxDesc.sd: The type of the default action.

properties.description:
#### Configures where to redirect the request.

---

A redirect changes the URI, which has the format: `protocol://hostname:port/path?query`.
Unmodified URI components will retain their original values.

To avoid redirect loops, ensure that you sufficiently modify the URI.
You can reuse URI components with the following keywords: `#{protocol}`, `#{host}`, `#{port}`, `#{path}` (the leading `/` is removed), and `#{query}`.

For example, you can change the path to `/new/#{path}`, the hostname to `example.#{host}`, or the query to `#{query}&value=xyz`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Configures where to redirect the request.
_MdxDesc.ld:
A redirect changes the URI, which has the format: protocol://hostname:port/path?query. Unmodified URI components will retain their original values.

To avoid redirect loops, ensure that you sufficiently modify the URI. You can reuse URI components with the following keywords: #{protocol}, #{host}, #{port}, #{path} (the leading / is removed), and #{query}.

For example, you can change the path to /new/#{path}, the hostname to example.#{host}, or the query to #{query}&value=xyz.

path.description: #### Redirect path (must start with `/`). Use `#{path}` to reuse the original path.

_MdxDesc.sd: Redirect path (must start with /). Use #{path} to reuse the original path.

query.description: #### Query string for the redirect (without leading `?`). Use `#{query}` to preserve the original.

_MdxDesc.sd: Query string for the redirect (without leading ?). Use #{query} to preserve the original.

port.description: #### Redirect port (1-65535). Use `#{port}` to keep the original.

_MdxDesc.sd: Redirect port (1-65535). Use #{port} to keep the original.

host.description: #### Redirect hostname. Use `#{host}` to keep the original.

_MdxDesc.sd: Redirect hostname. Use #{host} to keep the original.

protocol.description: #### Redirect protocol. Cannot redirect HTTPS to HTTP.

_MdxDesc.sd: Redirect protocol. Cannot redirect HTTPS to HTTP.
HTTP_301 (permanent) or HTTP_302 (temporary) redirect.

listenerPort.description: #### Listener port for CDN traffic. Only needed if using custom listeners.

_MdxDesc.sd: Listener port for CDN traffic. Only needed if using custom listeners.

originDomainName.description: #### Explicit origin domain. Only needed if the ALB has no `customDomains` and uses `customCertificateArns`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Explicit origin domain. Only needed if the ALB has no customDomains and uses customCertificateArns.

enabled.description:
#### Enable CDN (CloudFront) for faster global delivery and lower bandwidth costs.

---

Caches responses at edge locations worldwide so users get content from the nearest server.
The CDN itself has no monthly fee — you pay per request (~$0.01/10k) and per GB transferred.

_MdxDesc.sd: Enable CDN (CloudFront) for faster global delivery and lower bandwidth costs.
_MdxDesc.ld: Caches responses at edge locations worldwide so users get content from the nearest server. The CDN itself has no monthly fee — you pay per request (~$0.01/10k) and per GB transferred.

cachingOptions.description:
#### Control how long and what gets cached at the CDN edge.

---

When the origin response has no `Cache-Control` header, defaults apply:
- **Bucket origins**: cached for 6 months (or until invalidated on deploy).
- **API Gateway / Load Balancer origins**: not cached.

_MdxDesc.sd: Control how long and what gets cached at the CDN edge.
_MdxDesc.ld:
When the origin response has no Cache-Control header, defaults apply:

  • Bucket origins: cached for 6 months (or until invalidated on deploy).
  • API Gateway / Load Balancer origins: not cached.

forwardingOptions.description:
#### Control which headers, cookies, and query params are forwarded to your origin.

---

By default, all headers/cookies/query params are forwarded. Use this to restrict
what reaches your app (e.g., strip cookies for static content).

_MdxDesc.sd: Control which headers, cookies, and query params are forwarded to your origin.
_MdxDesc.ld: By default, all headers/cookies/query params are forwarded. Use this to restrict what reaches your app (e.g., strip cookies for static content).

routeRewrites.description:
#### Route specific URL patterns to different origins (e.g., `/api/*` → Lambda, `/assets/*` → S3).

---

Evaluated in order; first match wins. Unmatched requests go to the default origin.
Each route can have its own caching and forwarding settings.

_MdxDesc.sd: Route specific URL patterns to different origins (e.g., /api/* → Lambda, /assets/* → S3).
_MdxDesc.ld: Evaluated in order; first match wins. Unmatched requests go to the default origin. Each route can have its own caching and forwarding settings.

customDomains.description:
#### Custom domains (e.g., `cdn.example.com`). Stacktape auto-creates DNS records and TLS certificates.

---

Your domain must be added as a Route53 hosted zone in your AWS account first.

_MdxDesc.sd: Custom domains (e.g., cdn.example.com). Stacktape auto-creates DNS records and TLS certificates.
_MdxDesc.ld: Your domain must be added as a Route53 hosted zone in your AWS account first.

edgeFunctions.description:
#### Run edge functions on CDN requests/responses (URL rewrites, auth, A/B testing).

---

- `onRequest`: Before cache lookup — modify the request, add auth, or return early.
- `onResponse`: Before returning to the client — modify headers, add cookies.

_MdxDesc.sd: Run edge functions on CDN requests/responses (URL rewrites, auth, A/B testing).
_MdxDesc.ld:
• onRequest: Before cache lookup — modify the request, add auth, or return early.
  • onResponse: Before returning to the client — modify headers, add cookies.

cloudfrontPriceClass.description:
#### Which regions the CDN serves from. Fewer regions = lower cost, but slower for distant users.

---

- **`PriceClass_100`**: North America + Europe. Cheapest option. Good if your users are in the US/EU.
- **`PriceClass_200`**: Adds Asia, Middle East, Africa.
- **`PriceClass_All`** (default): All regions worldwide, including South America and Oceania.

The CDN itself has no monthly base cost - you only pay per request and per GB transferred.
The price class controls which edge locations are used, and some regions cost more per request.

_MdxDesc.sd: Which regions the CDN serves from. Fewer regions = lower cost, but slower for distant users.
_MdxDesc.ld:
• PriceClass_100: North America + Europe. Cheapest option. Good if your users are in the US/EU.
  • PriceClass_200: Adds Asia, Middle East, Africa.
  • PriceClass_All (default): All regions worldwide, including South America and Oceania.

The CDN itself has no monthly base cost - you only pay per request and per GB transferred. The price class controls which edge locations are used, and some regions cost more per request.

defaultRoutePrefix.description:
#### Prepend a path prefix to all requests forwarded to the origin.

---

E.g., with prefix `/v2`, a request for `/users` is forwarded to the origin as `/v2/users`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Prepend a path prefix to all requests forwarded to the origin.
_MdxDesc.ld: E.g., with prefix /v2, a request for /users is forwarded to the origin as /v2/users.

_MdxDesc.sd: Page to show for 404 errors (e.g., /error.html).
Page served for requests to /.

disableInvalidationAfterDeploy.description:
#### Skip clearing the CDN cache after each deploy.

---

By default, all cached content is flushed on every deploy so users see the latest version.
Set to `true` if you manage cache invalidation yourself or want to keep cached content between deploys.

_MdxDesc.sd: Skip clearing the CDN cache after each deploy.
_MdxDesc.ld: By default, all cached content is flushed on every deploy so users see the latest version. Set to true if you manage cache invalidation yourself or want to keep cached content between deploys.

useFirewall.description: #### Name of a `web-app-firewall` resource to protect this CDN from common web exploits.

_MdxDesc.sd: Name of a web-app-firewall resource to protect this CDN from common web exploits.

NetworkLoadBalancer.description:
#### TCP/TLS load balancer for non-HTTP traffic (MQTT, game servers, custom protocols).

---

Handles millions of connections with ultra-low latency. Use when you need raw TCP/TLS
instead of HTTP routing. Does not support CDN, firewall, or gradual deployments.

overrides.description:
#### Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

accessibilityMode.description:
#### Configures pre-defined accessibility modes for the bucket.

---

This allows you to easily configure the most common access patterns.

Available modes:
- `public-read-write`: Everyone can read from and write to the bucket.
- `public-read`: Everyone can read from the bucket. Only compute resources and entities with sufficient IAM permissions can write to it.
- `private` (default): Only compute resources and entities with sufficient IAM permissions can read from or write to the bucket.

For functions, batch jobs, and container workloads, you can grant the required IAM permissions using the `allowsAccessTo` or `iamRoleStatements` properties in their respective configurations.

_MdxDesc.sd: Configures pre-defined accessibility modes for the bucket.
_MdxDesc.ld:
This allows you to easily configure the most common access patterns.

Available modes:

  • public-read-write: Everyone can read from and write to the bucket.
  • public-read: Everyone can read from the bucket. Only compute resources and entities with sufficient IAM permissions can write to it.
  • private (default): Only compute resources and entities with sufficient IAM permissions can read from or write to the bucket.

For functions, batch jobs, and container workloads, you can grant the required IAM permissions using the allowsAccessTo or iamRoleStatements properties in their respective configurations.

rewriteRoutesForSinglePageApp.description:
#### Rewrites incoming requests to work for a single-page application.

---

The routing works as follows:
- If the path has an extension (e.g., `.css`, `.js`, `.png`), the request is not rewritten, and the appropriate file is returned.
- If the path does not have an extension, the request is routed to `index.html`.

This allows single-page applications to handle routing on the client side.

_MdxDesc.sd: Rewrites incoming requests to work for a single-page application.
_MdxDesc.ld:
The routing works as follows:

  • If the path has an extension (e.g., .css, .js, .png), the request is not rewritten, and the appropriate file is returned.
  • If the path does not have an extension, the request is routed to index.html.

This allows single-page applications to handle routing on the client side.

disableUrlNormalization.description:
#### Disables URL normalization.

---

URL normalization is enabled by default and is useful for serving HTML files from the bucket with clean URLs.

_MdxDesc.sd: Disables URL normalization.
_MdxDesc.ld: URL normalization is enabled by default and is useful for serving HTML files from the bucket with clean URLs.

enabled.description:
#### Enable CDN (CloudFront) for faster global delivery and lower bandwidth costs.

---

Caches responses at edge locations worldwide so users get content from the nearest server.
The CDN itself has no monthly fee — you pay per request (~$0.01/10k) and per GB transferred.

_MdxDesc.sd: Enable CDN (CloudFront) for faster global delivery and lower bandwidth costs.
_MdxDesc.ld: Caches responses at edge locations worldwide so users get content from the nearest server. The CDN itself has no monthly fee — you pay per request (~$0.01/10k) and per GB transferred.

cachingOptions.description:
#### Control how long and what gets cached at the CDN edge.

---

When the origin response has no `Cache-Control` header, defaults apply:
- **Bucket origins**: cached for 6 months (or until invalidated on deploy).
- **API Gateway / Load Balancer origins**: not cached.

_MdxDesc.sd: Control how long and what gets cached at the CDN edge.
_MdxDesc.ld:
When the origin response has no Cache-Control header, defaults apply:

  • Bucket origins: cached for 6 months (or until invalidated on deploy).
  • API Gateway / Load Balancer origins: not cached.

forwardingOptions.description:
#### Control which headers, cookies, and query params are forwarded to your origin.

---

By default, all headers/cookies/query params are forwarded. Use this to restrict
what reaches your app (e.g., strip cookies for static content).

_MdxDesc.sd: Control which headers, cookies, and query params are forwarded to your origin.
_MdxDesc.ld: By default, all headers/cookies/query params are forwarded. Use this to restrict what reaches your app (e.g., strip cookies for static content).

routeRewrites.description:
#### Route specific URL patterns to different origins (e.g., `/api/*` → Lambda, `/assets/*` → S3).

---

Evaluated in order; first match wins. Unmatched requests go to the default origin.
Each route can have its own caching and forwarding settings.

_MdxDesc.sd: Route specific URL patterns to different origins (e.g., /api/* → Lambda, /assets/* → S3).
_MdxDesc.ld: Evaluated in order; first match wins. Unmatched requests go to the default origin. Each route can have its own caching and forwarding settings.

customDomains.description:
#### Custom domains (e.g., `cdn.example.com`). Stacktape auto-creates DNS records and TLS certificates.

---

Your domain must be added as a Route53 hosted zone in your AWS account first.

_MdxDesc.sd: Custom domains (e.g., cdn.example.com). Stacktape auto-creates DNS records and TLS certificates.
_MdxDesc.ld: Your domain must be added as a Route53 hosted zone in your AWS account first.

edgeFunctions.description:
#### Run edge functions on CDN requests/responses (URL rewrites, auth, A/B testing).

---

- `onRequest`: Before cache lookup — modify the request, add auth, or return early.
- `onResponse`: Before returning to the client — modify headers, add cookies.

_MdxDesc.sd: Run edge functions on CDN requests/responses (URL rewrites, auth, A/B testing).
_MdxDesc.ld:
• onRequest: Before cache lookup — modify the request, add auth, or return early.
  • onResponse: Before returning to the client — modify headers, add cookies.

cloudfrontPriceClass.description:
#### Which regions the CDN serves from. Fewer regions = lower cost, but slower for distant users.

---

- **`PriceClass_100`**: North America + Europe. Cheapest option. Good if your users are in the US/EU.
- **`PriceClass_200`**: Adds Asia, Middle East, Africa.
- **`PriceClass_All`** (default): All regions worldwide, including South America and Oceania.

The CDN itself has no monthly base cost - you only pay per request and per GB transferred.
The price class controls which edge locations are used, and some regions cost more per request.

_MdxDesc.sd: Which regions the CDN serves from. Fewer regions = lower cost, but slower for distant users.
_MdxDesc.ld:
• PriceClass_100: North America + Europe. Cheapest option. Good if your users are in the US/EU.
  • PriceClass_200: Adds Asia, Middle East, Africa.
  • PriceClass_All (default): All regions worldwide, including South America and Oceania.

The CDN itself has no monthly base cost - you only pay per request and per GB transferred. The price class controls which edge locations are used, and some regions cost more per request.

defaultRoutePrefix.description:
#### Prepend a path prefix to all requests forwarded to the origin.

---

E.g., with prefix `/v2`, a request for `/users` is forwarded to the origin as `/v2/users`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Prepend a path prefix to all requests forwarded to the origin.
_MdxDesc.ld: E.g., with prefix /v2, a request for /users is forwarded to the origin as /v2/users.

_MdxDesc.sd: Page to show for 404 errors (e.g., /error.html).
Page served for requests to /.

disableInvalidationAfterDeploy.description:
#### Skip clearing the CDN cache after each deploy.

---

By default, all cached content is flushed on every deploy so users see the latest version.
Set to `true` if you manage cache invalidation yourself or want to keep cached content between deploys.

_MdxDesc.sd: Skip clearing the CDN cache after each deploy.
_MdxDesc.ld: By default, all cached content is flushed on every deploy so users see the latest version. Set to true if you manage cache invalidation yourself or want to keep cached content between deploys.

useFirewall.description: #### Name of a `web-app-firewall` resource to protect this CDN from common web exploits.

_MdxDesc.sd: Name of a web-app-firewall resource to protect this CDN from common web exploits.

UserAuthPool.description:
#### A resource for managing user authentication and authorization.

---

A user pool is a fully managed identity provider that handles user sign-up, sign-in, and access control.
It provides a secure and scalable way to manage user identities for your applications.

overrides.description:
#### Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.sd: A resource for managing user authentication and authorization.
_MdxDesc.ld: A user pool is a fully managed identity provider that handles user sign-up, sign-in, and access control. It provides a secure and scalable way to manage user identities for your applications.

allowOnlyAdminsToCreateAccount.description:
#### Restrict account creation to administrators

---

If enabled, new users can't sign up themselves. Accounts must be created through an admin flow (for example from an internal admin tool or script),
which helps prevent unwanted self-registrations.

Internally this controls `AdminCreateUserConfig.AllowAdminCreateUserOnly` on the Cognito user pool
([AWS::Cognito::UserPool](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/TemplateReference/aws-resource-cognito-userpool)).

_MdxDesc.sd: Restrict account creation to administrators
_MdxDesc.ld:
If enabled, new users can't sign up themselves. Accounts must be created through an admin flow (for example from an internal admin tool or script), which helps prevent unwanted self-registrations.

Internally this controls AdminCreateUserConfig.AllowAdminCreateUserOnly on the Cognito user pool (AWS::Cognito::UserPool).

unusedAccountValidityDays.description:
#### Expire unused admin-created accounts

---

When an admin creates a user account, Cognito issues a temporary password. This setting controls how many days that temporary password
(and the corresponding account) stays valid if the user never signs in.

Internally this maps to `AdminCreateUserConfig.UnusedAccountValidityDays`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Expire unused admin-created accounts
_MdxDesc.ld:
When an admin creates a user account, Cognito issues a temporary password. This setting controls how many days that temporary password (and the corresponding account) stays valid if the user never signs in.

Internally this maps to AdminCreateUserConfig.UnusedAccountValidityDays.

requireEmailVerification.description:
#### (Reserved) Require verified emails

---

Present for forward compatibility. Stacktape currently derives email / phone verification behavior from `userVerificationType`,
not from this flag directly.

To require email-based verification today, use `userVerificationType: 'email-link' | 'email-code'` instead.

_MdxDesc.sd: (Reserved) Require verified emails
_MdxDesc.ld:
Present for forward compatibility. Stacktape currently derives email / phone verification behavior from userVerificationType, not from this flag directly.

To require email-based verification today, use userVerificationType: 'email-link' | 'email-code' instead.

requirePhoneNumberVerification.description:
#### (Reserved) Require verified phone numbers

---

Present for forward compatibility. Stacktape currently derives email / phone verification behavior from `userVerificationType`,
not from this flag directly.

To require SMS-based verification today, use `userVerificationType: 'sms'`.

_MdxDesc.sd: (Reserved) Require verified phone numbers
_MdxDesc.ld:
Present for forward compatibility. Stacktape currently derives email / phone verification behavior from userVerificationType, not from this flag directly.

To require SMS-based verification today, use userVerificationType: 'sms'.

enableHostedUi.description:
#### Enable the Cognito Hosted UI

---

Turns on Cognito's Hosted UI – a pre-built, hosted login and registration page – so you don't have to build your own auth screens.
This is useful when you want to get started quickly or keep authentication logic outside of your main app.

_MdxDesc.sd: Enable the Cognito Hosted UI
_MdxDesc.ld: Turns on Cognito's Hosted UI – a pre-built, hosted login and registration page – so you don't have to build your own auth screens. This is useful when you want to get started quickly or keep authentication logic outside of your main app.

hostedUiDomainPrefix.description:
#### Hosted UI domain prefix

---

Sets the first part of your Hosted UI URL: `https://<prefix>.auth.<region>.amazoncognito.com`.
Pick something that matches your project or company name.

_MdxDesc.sd: Hosted UI domain prefix
_MdxDesc.ld: Sets the first part of your Hosted UI URL: https://.auth..amazoncognito.com. Pick something that matches your project or company name.

hostedUiCSS.description:
#### Custom CSS for the Hosted UI

---

Lets you override the default Cognito Hosted UI styling with your own CSS (colors, fonts, layouts, etc.),
so the login experience matches the rest of your application.

Behind the scenes this is applied using the `AWS::Cognito::UserPoolUICustomizationAttachment` resource.

_MdxDesc.sd: Custom CSS for the Hosted UI
_MdxDesc.ld:
Lets you override the default Cognito Hosted UI styling with your own CSS (colors, fonts, layouts, etc.), so the login experience matches the rest of your application.

Behind the scenes this is applied using the AWS::Cognito::UserPoolUICustomizationAttachment resource.

hooks.description:
#### Lambda triggers for the user pool

---

Connects AWS Lambda functions to Cognito "hooks" (triggers) such as pre-sign-up, post-confirmation, or token generation.
You can use these to enforce additional validation, enrich user profiles, migrate users from another system, and more.

Internally this maps to the Cognito user pool `LambdaConfig`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Lambda triggers for the user pool
_MdxDesc.ld:
Connects AWS Lambda functions to Cognito "hooks" (triggers) such as pre-sign-up, post-confirmation, or token generation. You can use these to enforce additional validation, enrich user profiles, migrate users from another system, and more.

Internally this maps to the Cognito user pool LambdaConfig.

emailConfiguration.description:
#### Email delivery settings

---

Controls how Cognito sends emails (verification messages, password reset codes, admin invitations, etc.).
You can either use Cognito's built-in email service or plug in your own SES identity for full control over the sender.

This config is used to build the Cognito `EmailConfiguration` block
([AWS docs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/TemplateReference/aws-resource-cognito-userpool)).

_MdxDesc.sd: Email delivery settings
_MdxDesc.ld:
Controls how Cognito sends emails (verification messages, password reset codes, admin invitations, etc.). You can either use Cognito's built-in email service or plug in your own SES identity for full control over the sender.

This config is used to build the Cognito EmailConfiguration block (AWS docs).

inviteMessageConfig.description:
#### Invite message overrides

---

Customizes the contents of the "invitation" message that users receive when an administrator creates their account
(for example, when sending a temporary password and sign-in instructions).

If you want to send custom emails through SES, you must also configure `emailConfiguration.sesAddressArn`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Invite message overrides
_MdxDesc.ld:
Customizes the contents of the "invitation" message that users receive when an administrator creates their account (for example, when sending a temporary password and sign-in instructions).

If you want to send custom emails through SES, you must also configure emailConfiguration.sesAddressArn.

userVerificationType.description:
#### Verification strategy

---

Chooses how new users prove that they own their contact information:

- `email-link`: Cognito emails a clickable link.
- `email-code`: Cognito emails a short numeric code.
- `sms`: Cognito sends a code via SMS to the user's phone number.
- `none`: Users aren't required to verify email or phone during sign-up.

Stacktape uses this value to configure `AutoVerifiedAttributes` and `VerificationMessageTemplate`
on the underlying Cognito user pool.

_MdxDesc.sd: Verification strategy
_MdxDesc.ld:
Chooses how new users prove that they own their contact information:

  • email-link: Cognito emails a clickable link.
  • email-code: Cognito emails a short numeric code.
  • sms: Cognito sends a code via SMS to the user's phone number.
  • none: Users aren't required to verify email or phone during sign-up.

Stacktape uses this value to configure AutoVerifiedAttributes and VerificationMessageTemplate on the underlying Cognito user pool.

userVerificationMessageConfig.description:
#### Verification message text

---

Lets you customize the exact email and SMS texts that Cognito sends when asking users to verify their email / phone.
For example, you can change subjects, body text, or the message that contains the `{####}` verification code.

_MdxDesc.sd: Verification message text
_MdxDesc.ld: Lets you customize the exact email and SMS texts that Cognito sends when asking users to verify their email / phone. For example, you can change subjects, body text, or the message that contains the {####} verification code.

mfaConfiguration.description:
#### Multi-factor authentication

---

Controls whether you use Multi‑Factor Authentication (MFA) and which second factors are allowed.
MFA makes it much harder for attackers to access accounts even if they know a user's password.

Under the hood this config drives both the `MfaConfiguration` and `EnabledMfas` properties in Cognito
(see "MFA configuration" in the
[AWS::Cognito::UserPool docs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/TemplateReference/aws-resource-cognito-userpool)).

_MdxDesc.sd: Multi-factor authentication
_MdxDesc.ld:
Controls whether you use Multi‑Factor Authentication (MFA) and which second factors are allowed. MFA makes it much harder for attackers to access accounts even if they know a user's password.

Under the hood this config drives both the MfaConfiguration and EnabledMfas properties in Cognito (see "MFA configuration" in the AWS::Cognito::UserPool docs).

passwordPolicy.description:
#### Password strength rules

---

Defines how strong user passwords must be – minimum length and whether they must include lowercase, uppercase,
numbers, and/or symbols – plus how long temporary passwords issued to new users remain valid.

This is applied to the Cognito `Policies.PasswordPolicy` block.

_MdxDesc.sd: Password strength rules
_MdxDesc.ld:
Defines how strong user passwords must be – minimum length and whether they must include lowercase, uppercase, numbers, and/or symbols – plus how long temporary passwords issued to new users remain valid.

This is applied to the Cognito Policies.PasswordPolicy block.

schema.description:
#### Custom attributes schema

---

Lets you define additional attributes (like `role`, `plan`, `companyId`, etc.) that are stored on each user,
including their data type and validation constraints.

These translate into the Cognito user pool `Schema` entries
([schema docs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/TemplateReference/aws-resource-cognito-userpool)).

_MdxDesc.sd: Custom attributes schema
_MdxDesc.ld:
Lets you define additional attributes (like role, plan, companyId, etc.) that are stored on each user, including their data type and validation constraints.

These translate into the Cognito user pool Schema entries (schema docs).

allowPhoneNumberAsUserName.description:
#### Allow phone numbers as usernames

---

If enabled (the default), users can sign in using their phone number in addition to any traditional username.
Turning this off means phone numbers can still be stored, but can't be used to log in.

This is implemented via Cognito's `UsernameAttributes` configuration.

_MdxDesc.sd: Allow phone numbers as usernames
_MdxDesc.ld:
If enabled (the default), users can sign in using their phone number in addition to any traditional username. Turning this off means phone numbers can still be stored, but can't be used to log in.

This is implemented via Cognito's UsernameAttributes configuration.

allowEmailAsUserName.description:
#### Allow email addresses as usernames

---

If enabled (the default), users can sign in using their email address instead of a dedicated username.
Turning this off means emails can still be stored, but can't be used to log in directly.

This is also controlled through Cognito `UsernameAttributes`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Allow email addresses as usernames
_MdxDesc.ld:
If enabled (the default), users can sign in using their email address instead of a dedicated username. Turning this off means emails can still be stored, but can't be used to log in directly.

This is also controlled through Cognito UsernameAttributes.

accessTokenValiditySeconds.description:
#### Access token lifetime

---

Controls how long an access token issued by Cognito stays valid after login. Shorter lifetimes reduce the window
in which a stolen token can be abused, at the cost of more frequent refreshes.

This value is passed to the user pool client as `AccessTokenValidity`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Access token lifetime
_MdxDesc.ld:
Controls how long an access token issued by Cognito stays valid after login. Shorter lifetimes reduce the window in which a stolen token can be abused, at the cost of more frequent refreshes.

This value is passed to the user pool client as AccessTokenValidity.

idTokenValiditySeconds.description:
#### ID token lifetime

---

Controls how long an ID token (which contains user profile and claims) is accepted before clients must obtain a new one.

This is set on the user pool client as `IdTokenValidity`.

_MdxDesc.sd: ID token lifetime
_MdxDesc.ld:
Controls how long an ID token (which contains user profile and claims) is accepted before clients must obtain a new one.

This is set on the user pool client as IdTokenValidity.

refreshTokenValidityDays.description:
#### Refresh token lifetime

---

Sets for how many days a refresh token can be used to obtain new access / ID tokens without requiring the user to sign in again.
Longer lifetimes mean fewer re-authentications, but keep sessions alive for longer.

This value is used as `RefreshTokenValidity` on the Cognito user pool client.

_MdxDesc.sd: Refresh token lifetime
_MdxDesc.ld:
Sets for how many days a refresh token can be used to obtain new access / ID tokens without requiring the user to sign in again. Longer lifetimes mean fewer re-authentications, but keep sessions alive for longer.

This value is used as RefreshTokenValidity on the Cognito user pool client.

allowedOAuthFlows.description:
#### OAuth flows

---

Specifies which OAuth 2.0 flows the user pool client is allowed to use:

- `code`: Authorization Code flow (recommended for web apps and backends).
- `implicit`: Implicit flow (legacy browser-only flow).
- `client_credentials`: Server‑to‑server (no end user) machine credentials.

These values populate `AllowedOAuthFlows` on the Cognito user pool client
([AWS::Cognito::UserPoolClient](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/TemplateReference/aws-resource-cognito-userpoolclient)).

_MdxDesc.sd: OAuth flows
_MdxDesc.ld:
Specifies which OAuth 2.0 flows the user pool client is allowed to use:

  • code: Authorization Code flow (recommended for web apps and backends).
  • implicit: Implicit flow (legacy browser-only flow).
  • client_credentials: Server‑to‑server (no end user) machine credentials.

These values populate AllowedOAuthFlows on the Cognito user pool client (AWS::Cognito::UserPoolClient).

allowedOAuthScopes.description:
#### OAuth scopes

---

Lists which scopes clients can request when using OAuth (for example `email`, `openid`, `profile`).
Scopes control which user information and permissions your app receives in tokens.

These values are passed to the user pool client as `AllowedOAuthScopes`.

_MdxDesc.sd: OAuth scopes
_MdxDesc.ld:
Lists which scopes clients can request when using OAuth (for example email, openid, profile). Scopes control which user information and permissions your app receives in tokens.

These values are passed to the user pool client as AllowedOAuthScopes.

callbackURLs.description:
#### OAuth callback URLs

---

The allowed URLs where Cognito is permitted to redirect users after successful authentication.
These must exactly match the URLs registered with your frontend / backend, otherwise the redirect will fail.

Mapped into `CallbackURLs` and `DefaultRedirectURI` on the user pool client.

_MdxDesc.sd: OAuth callback URLs
_MdxDesc.ld:
The allowed URLs where Cognito is permitted to redirect users after successful authentication. These must exactly match the URLs registered with your frontend / backend, otherwise the redirect will fail.

Mapped into CallbackURLs and DefaultRedirectURI on the user pool client.

logoutURLs.description:
#### OAuth logout URLs

---

The URLs Cognito can redirect users to after they log out of the Hosted UI or end their session.
Must also be explicitly configured so that sign-out redirects don't fail.

These populate the `LogoutURLs` list on the user pool client.

_MdxDesc.sd: OAuth logout URLs
_MdxDesc.ld:
The URLs Cognito can redirect users to after they log out of the Hosted UI or end their session. Must also be explicitly configured so that sign-out redirects don't fail.

These populate the LogoutURLs list on the user pool client.

identityProviders.description:
#### External identity providers

---

Allows users to sign in with third‑party identity providers like Google, Facebook, Login with Amazon, OIDC, SAML, or Sign in with Apple.
Each entry configures one external provider (client ID/secret, attribute mapping, requested scopes, and advanced provider‑specific options).

Under the hood Stacktape creates separate `AWS::Cognito::UserPoolIdentityProvider` resources and registers them
in the user pool client's `SupportedIdentityProviders`.

_MdxDesc.sd: External identity providers
_MdxDesc.ld:
Allows users to sign in with third‑party identity providers like Google, Facebook, Login with Amazon, OIDC, SAML, or Sign in with Apple. Each entry configures one external provider (client ID/secret, attribute mapping, requested scopes, and advanced provider‑specific options).

Under the hood Stacktape creates separate AWS::Cognito::UserPoolIdentityProvider resources and registers them in the user pool client's SupportedIdentityProviders.

useFirewall.description:
#### Associate a WAF

---

Links the user pool to a `web-app-firewall` resource, so requests to the Hosted UI and token endpoints are inspected
by AWS WAF rules you configure in Stacktape.

Stacktape does this by creating a `WebACLAssociation` between the user pool and the referenced firewall.

_MdxDesc.sd: Associate a WAF
_MdxDesc.ld:
Links the user pool to a web-app-firewall resource, so requests to the Hosted UI and token endpoints are inspected by AWS WAF rules you configure in Stacktape.

Stacktape does this by creating a WebACLAssociation between the user pool and the referenced firewall.

customDomain.description:
#### Custom Domain

---

Configures a custom domain for the Cognito Hosted UI (e.g., `auth.example.com`).

When configured, Cognito creates a CloudFront distribution to serve your custom domain.
Stacktape automatically:
- Configures the user pool domain with your custom domain and an ACM certificate from us-east-1
- Creates a DNS record pointing to the CloudFront distribution

The domain must be registered and verified in your Stacktape account with a valid ACM certificate in us-east-1.

_MdxDesc.sd: Custom Domain
_MdxDesc.ld:
Configures a custom domain for the Cognito Hosted UI (e.g., auth.example.com).

When configured, Cognito creates a CloudFront distribution to serve your custom domain. Stacktape automatically:

  • Configures the user pool domain with your custom domain and an ACM certificate from us-east-1
  • Creates a DNS record pointing to the CloudFront distribution

The domain must be registered and verified in your Stacktape account with a valid ACM certificate in us-east-1.

generateClientSecret.description:
#### Generate a client secret

---

Asks Cognito to generate a secret for the user pool client. Use this when you have trusted backends (like APIs or server‑side apps)
that can safely store a client secret and use confidential OAuth flows.

This flag controls the `GenerateSecret` property on the user pool client.

_MdxDesc.sd: Generate a client secret
_MdxDesc.ld:
Asks Cognito to generate a secret for the user pool client. Use this when you have trusted backends (like APIs or server‑side apps) that can safely store a client secret and use confidential OAuth flows.

This flag controls the GenerateSecret property on the user pool client.

allowOnlyExternalIdentityProviders.description:
#### Force external identity providers

---

If `true`, users can't sign in with a username/password against the Cognito user directory at all.
Instead, they must always use one of the configured external identity providers (Google, SAML, etc.).

Internally this removes `COGNITO` from `SupportedIdentityProviders` on the user pool client.

_MdxDesc.sd: Force external identity providers
_MdxDesc.ld:
If true, users can't sign in with a username/password against the Cognito user directory at all. Instead, they must always use one of the configured external identity providers (Google, SAML, etc.).

Internally this removes COGNITO from SupportedIdentityProviders on the user pool client.

customMessage.description:
#### Custom message hook

Triggered whenever Cognito is about to send an email or SMS (sign‑up, verification, password reset, etc.).
Lets you fully customize message contents or dynamically choose language/branding.

Value must be the ARN of a Lambda function configured to handle the "Custom Message" trigger.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Custom message hook

Triggered whenever Cognito is about to send an email or SMS (sign‑up, verification, password reset, etc.). Lets you fully customize message contents or dynamically choose language/branding.

Value must be the ARN of a Lambda function configured to handle the "Custom Message" trigger.

postAuthentication.description:
#### Post-authentication hook

Runs after a user has successfully authenticated. You can use this to record analytics, update last‑login timestamps,
or block access based on additional checks.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Post-authentication hook

Runs after a user has successfully authenticated. You can use this to record analytics, update last‑login timestamps, or block access based on additional checks.

postConfirmation.description:
#### Post-confirmation hook

Runs right after a user confirms their account (for example via email link or admin confirmation).
This is often used to create user records in your own database or to provision resources.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Post-confirmation hook

Runs right after a user confirms their account (for example via email link or admin confirmation). This is often used to create user records in your own database or to provision resources.

preAuthentication.description:
#### Pre-authentication hook

Invoked just before Cognito validates a user's credentials. You can use this to block sign‑in attempts
based on IP, device, or user state (for example, soft‑deleting an account).

_MdxDesc.sd:
Pre-authentication hook

Invoked just before Cognito validates a user's credentials. You can use this to block sign‑in attempts based on IP, device, or user state (for example, soft‑deleting an account).

preSignUp.description:
#### Pre-sign-up hook

Called before a new user is created. Useful for validating input, auto‑confirming trusted users,
or blocking sign‑ups that don't meet your business rules.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Pre-sign-up hook

Called before a new user is created. Useful for validating input, auto‑confirming trusted users, or blocking sign‑ups that don't meet your business rules.

preTokenGeneration.description:
#### Pre-token-generation hook

Runs right before Cognito issues tokens. Lets you customize token claims (for example, adding roles or flags)
based on external systems or additional logic.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Pre-token-generation hook

Runs right before Cognito issues tokens. Lets you customize token claims (for example, adding roles or flags) based on external systems or additional logic.

userMigration.description:
#### User migration hook

Lets you migrate users on‑the‑fly from another user store. When someone tries to sign in but doesn't exist in Cognito,
this trigger can look them up elsewhere, import them, and let the sign‑in continue.

_MdxDesc.sd:
User migration hook

Lets you migrate users on‑the‑fly from another user store. When someone tries to sign in but doesn't exist in Cognito, this trigger can look them up elsewhere, import them, and let the sign‑in continue.

createAuthChallenge.description:
#### Create auth challenge hook

Part of Cognito's custom auth flow. This trigger is used to generate a challenge (for example sending a custom OTP)
after `DefineAuthChallenge` decides a challenge is needed.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Create auth challenge hook

Part of Cognito's custom auth flow. This trigger is used to generate a challenge (for example sending a custom OTP) after DefineAuthChallenge decides a challenge is needed.

defineAuthChallenge.description:
#### Define auth challenge hook

Also part of the custom auth flow. It decides whether a user needs another challenge, has passed, or has failed,
based on previous challenges and responses.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Define auth challenge hook

Also part of the custom auth flow. It decides whether a user needs another challenge, has passed, or has failed, based on previous challenges and responses.

verifyAuthChallengeResponse.description:
#### Verify auth challenge response hook

Validates the user's response to a custom challenge (for example, checking an OTP the user provides).

_MdxDesc.sd:
Verify auth challenge response hook

Validates the user's response to a custom challenge (for example, checking an OTP the user provides).

sesAddressArn.description:
#### SES identity to send emails from

---

ARN of an SES verified identity (email address or domain) that Cognito should use when sending emails.
Required when you want full control over sending (for example for MFA via `EMAIL_OTP`), because Cognito
must switch into `DEVELOPER` email sending mode.

_MdxDesc.sd: SES identity to send emails from
_MdxDesc.ld: ARN of an SES verified identity (email address or domain) that Cognito should use when sending emails. Required when you want full control over sending (for example for MFA via EMAIL_OTP), because Cognito must switch into DEVELOPER email sending mode.

from.description:
#### From address

---

The email address that appears in the "From" field of messages sent by Cognito (if you're using SES).
This address must be verified in SES if you're sending through your own identity.

_MdxDesc.sd: From address
_MdxDesc.ld: The email address that appears in the "From" field of messages sent by Cognito (if you're using SES). This address must be verified in SES if you're sending through your own identity.

replyToEmailAddress.description:
#### Reply-to address

---

Optional address where replies to Cognito emails should be delivered.
If not set, replies go to the `from` address (or the default Cognito sender).

_MdxDesc.sd: Reply-to address
_MdxDesc.ld: Optional address where replies to Cognito emails should be delivered. If not set, replies go to the from address (or the default Cognito sender).

emailMessage.description:
#### Invitation email body

---

The text of the email sent when an administrator creates a new user.
You can reference placeholders like `{username}` and `{####}` (temporary password or code) in the message.

_MdxDesc.sd: Invitation email body
_MdxDesc.ld: The text of the email sent when an administrator creates a new user. You can reference placeholders like {username} and {####} (temporary password or code) in the message.

_MdxDesc.sd: Invitation email subject

smsMessage.description:
#### Invitation SMS body

---

The content of the SMS message sent when new users are created with a phone number.
As with email, you can include placeholders such as `{username}` and `{####}`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Invitation SMS body
_MdxDesc.ld: The content of the SMS message sent when new users are created with a phone number. As with email, you can include placeholders such as {username} and {####}.

emailMessageUsingCode.description:
#### Email body when verifying with a code

Used when `userVerificationType` is `email-code`. The message typically contains a `{####}` placeholder
that Cognito replaces with a one‑time verification code.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Email body when verifying with a code

Used when userVerificationType is email-code. The message typically contains a {####} placeholder that Cognito replaces with a one‑time verification code.

emailMessageUsingLink.description:
#### Email body when verifying with a link

Used when `userVerificationType` is `email-link`. Cognito replaces special markers like `{##verify your email##}`
with a clickable URL.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Email body when verifying with a link

Used when userVerificationType is email-link. Cognito replaces special markers like {##verify your email##} with a clickable URL.

emailSubjectUsingCode.description: #### Email subject when verifying with a code

_MdxDesc.sd: Email subject when verifying with a code

emailSubjectUsingLink.description: #### Email subject when verifying with a link

_MdxDesc.sd: Email subject when verifying with a link

smsMessage.description:
#### SMS verification message

---

Text of the SMS Cognito sends when verifying a phone number (for example containing `{####}`).

_MdxDesc.sd: SMS verification message
_MdxDesc.ld: Text of the SMS Cognito sends when verifying a phone number (for example containing {####}).

status.description:
#### MFA requirement

- `OFF`: MFA is completely disabled.
- `ON`: All users must complete MFA during sign‑in.
- `OPTIONAL`: Users can opt in to MFA; it's recommended but not strictly required.

This value configures the Cognito `MfaConfiguration` property.

_MdxDesc.sd:
MFA requirement

  • OFF: MFA is completely disabled.
  • ON: All users must complete MFA during sign‑in.
  • OPTIONAL: Users can opt in to MFA; it's recommended but not strictly required.

This value configures the Cognito MfaConfiguration property.

enabledTypes.description:
#### Enabled MFA factor types

---

Chooses which MFA methods users can use:

- `SMS`: One‑time codes are sent via text message. Requires an SNS role so Cognito can send SMS.
- `SOFTWARE_TOKEN`: Time‑based one‑time codes from an authenticator app.
- `EMAIL_OTP`: Codes are sent by email. AWS requires that you configure a developer email sending identity
  (which Stacktape does when you provide `emailConfiguration.sesAddressArn`).

These values are mapped to Cognito's `EnabledMfas` setting (`SMS_MFA`, `SOFTWARE_TOKEN_MFA`, `EMAIL_OTP`),
whose behavior is described in
[EnabledMfas in the AWS::Cognito::UserPool docs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/TemplateReference/aws-resource-cognito-userpool).

_MdxDesc.sd: Enabled MFA factor types
_MdxDesc.ld:
Chooses which MFA methods users can use:

  • SMS: One‑time codes are sent via text message. Requires an SNS role so Cognito can send SMS.
  • SOFTWARE_TOKEN: Time‑based one‑time codes from an authenticator app.
  • EMAIL_OTP: Codes are sent by email. AWS requires that you configure a developer email sending identity (which Stacktape does when you provide emailConfiguration.sesAddressArn).

These values are mapped to Cognito's EnabledMfas setting (SMS_MFA, SOFTWARE_TOKEN_MFA, EMAIL_OTP), whose behavior is described in EnabledMfas in the AWS::Cognito::UserPool docs.

minimumLength.description:
#### Minimum password length

The fewest characters a password can have. Longer passwords are generally more secure.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Minimum password length

The fewest characters a password can have. Longer passwords are generally more secure.

requireLowercase.description: #### Require at least one lowercase letter

_MdxDesc.sd: Require at least one lowercase letter
Require at least one number

requireSymbols.description:
#### Require at least one symbol

Symbols are non‑alphanumeric characters such as `!`, `@`, or `#`.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Require at least one symbol

Symbols are non‑alphanumeric characters such as !, @, or #.

requireUppercase.description: #### Require at least one uppercase letter

_MdxDesc.sd: Require at least one uppercase letter

temporaryPasswordValidityDays.description:
#### Temporary password validity (days)

How long a temporary password issued to a new user is valid before it must be changed on first sign‑in.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Temporary password validity (days)

How long a temporary password issued to a new user is valid before it must be changed on first sign‑in.

name.description:
#### Attribute name

The logical name of the attribute as it appears on the user (for example `given_name`, `plan`, or `tenantId`).

_MdxDesc.sd:
Attribute name

The logical name of the attribute as it appears on the user (for example given_name, plan, or tenantId).

attributeDataType.description:
#### Attribute data type

The value type stored for this attribute (`String`, `Number`, etc.).
This is passed to Cognito's `AttributeDataType`.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Attribute data type

The value type stored for this attribute (String, Number, etc.). This is passed to Cognito's AttributeDataType.

developerOnlyAttribute.description:
#### Developer-only attribute

If true, the attribute is only readable/writable by privileged backend code and not exposed to end users directly.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Developer-only attribute

If true, the attribute is only readable/writable by privileged backend code and not exposed to end users directly.

mutable.description:
#### Mutable after sign-up

Controls whether the attribute can be changed after it has been initially set.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Mutable after sign-up

Controls whether the attribute can be changed after it has been initially set.

required.description:
#### Required at sign-up

If true, users must supply this attribute when creating an account.

_MdxDesc.sd:
Required at sign-up

If true, users must supply this attribute when creating an account.
Maximum numeric value
Minimum numeric value
Maximum string length
Minimum string length

type.description:
#### Provider type

---

The kind of external identity provider you want to integrate:

- `Facebook`, `Google`, `LoginWithAmazon`, `SignInWithApple`: social identity providers.
- `OIDC`: a generic OpenID Connect provider.
- `SAML`: a SAML 2.0 identity provider (often used for enterprise SSO).

_MdxDesc.sd: Provider type
_MdxDesc.ld:
The kind of external identity provider you want to integrate:

  • Facebook, Google, LoginWithAmazon, SignInWithApple: social identity providers.
  • OIDC: a generic OpenID Connect provider.
  • SAML: a SAML 2.0 identity provider (often used for enterprise SSO).

clientId.description:
#### OAuth / OIDC client ID

---

The client ID (sometimes called app ID) that you obtained from the external provider's console.
Cognito presents this ID when redirecting users to the provider.

_MdxDesc.sd: OAuth / OIDC client ID
_MdxDesc.ld: The client ID (sometimes called app ID) that you obtained from the external provider's console. Cognito presents this ID when redirecting users to the provider.

clientSecret.description:
#### OAuth / OIDC client secret

---

The client secret associated with the `clientId`, used by Cognito when exchanging authorization codes for tokens.
This value should be kept confidential and only configured from secure sources.

_MdxDesc.sd: OAuth / OIDC client secret
_MdxDesc.ld: The client secret associated with the clientId, used by Cognito when exchanging authorization codes for tokens. This value should be kept confidential and only configured from secure sources.

attributeMapping.description:
#### Attribute mapping

---

Maps attributes from the external provider (for example `email`, `given_name`) to Cognito user pool attributes.
Keys are Cognito attribute names, values are attribute names from the identity provider.

If not provided, Stacktape defaults to mapping `email -> email`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Attribute mapping
_MdxDesc.ld:
Maps attributes from the external provider (for example email, given_name) to Cognito user pool attributes. Keys are Cognito attribute names, values are attribute names from the identity provider.

If not provided, Stacktape defaults to mapping email -> email.

authorizeScopes.description:
#### Requested scopes

---

Additional OAuth scopes to request from the identity provider (for example `openid`, `email`, `profile`).
These control which pieces of user information and permissions your app receives in the provider's tokens.

If omitted, Stacktape uses a reasonable default per provider (see
[AWS::Cognito::UserPoolIdentityProvider](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/TemplateReference/aws-resource-cognito-userpoolidentityprovider)).

_MdxDesc.sd: Requested scopes
_MdxDesc.ld:
Additional OAuth scopes to request from the identity provider (for example openid, email, profile). These control which pieces of user information and permissions your app receives in the provider's tokens.

If omitted, Stacktape uses a reasonable default per provider (see AWS::Cognito::UserPoolIdentityProvider).

providerDetails.description:
#### Advanced provider options

---

Low‑level configuration passed directly into Cognito's `ProviderDetails` map.
You can use this to override endpoints or supply provider‑specific keys as documented by AWS,
for example `authorize_url`, `token_url`, `attributes_request_method`, `oidc_issuer`, and others.

In most cases you don't need to set this – Stacktape configures sensible defaults for common providers.

_MdxDesc.sd: Advanced provider options
_MdxDesc.ld:
Low‑level configuration passed directly into Cognito's ProviderDetails map. You can use this to override endpoints or supply provider‑specific keys as documented by AWS, for example authorize_url, token_url, attributes_request_method, oidc_issuer, and others.

In most cases you don't need to set this – Stacktape configures sensible defaults for common providers.

domainName.description:
#### Domain Name

---

Fully qualified domain name for the Cognito Hosted UI (e.g., `auth.example.com`).

_MdxDesc.sd: Domain Name
_MdxDesc.ld: Fully qualified domain name for the Cognito Hosted UI (e.g., auth.example.com).

customCertificateArn.description:
#### Custom Certificate ARN

---

ARN of an ACM certificate in **us-east-1** to use for this domain.
By default, Stacktape uses the certificate associated with your domain in us-east-1.

The certificate must be in us-east-1 because Cognito uses CloudFront for custom domains.

_MdxDesc.sd: Custom Certificate ARN
_MdxDesc.ld:
ARN of an ACM certificate in us-east-1 to use for this domain. By default, Stacktape uses the certificate associated with your domain in us-east-1.

The certificate must be in us-east-1 because Cognito uses CloudFront for custom domains.

disableDnsRecordCreation.description:
#### Disable DNS Record Creation

---

If `true`, Stacktape will not create a DNS record for this domain.

_MdxDesc.sd: Disable DNS Record Creation
_MdxDesc.ld: If true, Stacktape will not create a DNS record for this domain.

EventBus.description:
#### Central event bus for decoupling services. Publish events and trigger functions, queues, or batch jobs.

---

Use to build event-driven architectures where producers and consumers are independent.
Functions, batch jobs, and other resources can subscribe to specific event patterns.

overrides.description:

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.sd: Central event bus for decoupling services. Publish events and trigger functions, queues, or batch jobs.
_MdxDesc.ld: Use to build event-driven architectures where producers and consumers are independent. Functions, batch jobs, and other resources can subscribe to specific event patterns.

eventSourceName.description: #### Partner event source name. Only needed for receiving events from third-party SaaS integrations.

archivation.description: #### Archive events to store and replay them later. Useful for debugging, testing, or error recovery.

enabled.description: #### Enable event archiving. Disabling deletes the archive.

retentionDays.description: #### Days to keep archived events. Omit to keep indefinitely.

_MdxDesc.sd: Days to keep archived events. Omit to keep indefinitely.

Bastion.description:
#### Secure jump box for accessing private resources (databases, Redis, OpenSearch) in your VPC.

---

Uses keyless SSH via AWS Systems Manager — no SSH keys to manage. Connect with `stacktape bastion:ssh`
or create port-forwarding tunnels with `stacktape bastion:tunnel`. Costs ~$4/month (t3.micro).

overrides.description:

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.sd: Secure jump box for accessing private resources (databases, Redis, OpenSearch) in your VPC.

instanceSize.description: #### EC2 instance type. `t3.micro` is sufficient for SSH tunneling and basic admin tasks.

runCommandsAtLaunch.description:
#### Shell commands to run when the instance starts (as root — no `sudo` needed).

---

Use to install CLI tools, database clients, or other dependencies.
**Warning:** changing this list after creation replaces the instance — any data on the old instance is lost.

logging.description: #### Log retention settings for system, security, and audit logs. Logs are sent to CloudWatch.

_MdxDesc.sd: Log retention settings for system, security, and audit logs. Logs are sent to CloudWatch.

messages.description: #### System messages (`/var/log/messages`) — startup info, kernel messages, service logs.

_MdxDesc.sd: System messages (/var/log/messages) — startup info, kernel messages, service logs.

secure.description: #### Auth logs (`/var/log/secure`) — SSH login attempts, authentication events.

_MdxDesc.sd: Auth logs (/var/log/secure) — SSH login attempts, authentication events.

audit.description: #### Audit logs (`/var/log/audit/audit.log`) — detailed security events from the Linux audit system.

_MdxDesc.sd: Audit logs (/var/log/audit/audit.log) — detailed security events from the Linux audit system.

disabled.description: #### Disable this log type. Stops sending to CloudWatch.

retentionDays.description: #### Days to keep logs in CloudWatch before automatic deletion.

_MdxDesc.sd: Days to keep logs in CloudWatch before automatic deletion.

logForwarding.description:
#### Forward logs to an external service (Datadog, Highlight.io, or any HTTP endpoint).

---

Uses Kinesis Data Firehose (~$0.03/GB). Failed deliveries go to a backup S3 bucket.

DynamoDbTable.description:
#### Serverless NoSQL database with single-digit millisecond reads/writes at any scale.

---

No servers to manage, no capacity planning needed (in on-demand mode). Pay per read/write.
Great for user profiles, session data, IoT data, and any key-value or document workload.

overrides.description:
#### Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.sd: Serverless NoSQL database with single-digit millisecond reads/writes at any scale.
_MdxDesc.ld: No servers to manage, no capacity planning needed (in on-demand mode). Pay per read/write. Great for user profiles, session data, IoT data, and any key-value or document workload.

primaryKey.description:
#### The primary key that uniquely identifies each item.

---

- **Simple key**: Just a `partitionKey` (e.g., `userId`).
- **Composite key**: `partitionKey` + `sortKey` (e.g., `userId` + `createdAt`).

> **Cannot be changed after creation.** Every item must include the primary key attribute(s).

_MdxDesc.sd: The primary key that uniquely identifies each item.
_MdxDesc.ld:
• Simple key: Just a partitionKey (e.g., userId).
  • Composite key: partitionKey + sortKey (e.g., userId + createdAt).

Cannot be changed after creation. Every item must include the primary key attribute(s).

provisionedThroughput.description:
#### Fixed-capacity mode with predictable pricing. Omit for on-demand (pay-per-request) mode.

---

- **On-demand** (default, no config): Pay per read/write. Best for unpredictable or variable traffic.
- **Provisioned**: Set fixed read/write capacity. Cheaper at steady, predictable load. Can auto-scale.

_MdxDesc.sd: Fixed-capacity mode with predictable pricing. Omit for on-demand (pay-per-request) mode.
_MdxDesc.ld:
• On-demand (default, no config): Pay per read/write. Best for unpredictable or variable traffic.
  • Provisioned: Set fixed read/write capacity. Cheaper at steady, predictable load. Can auto-scale.

enablePointInTimeRecovery.description:
#### Enable continuous backups with point-in-time recovery (restore to any second in the last 35 days).

---

Restores always create a new table. Adds ~20% to storage cost.

_MdxDesc.sd: Enable continuous backups with point-in-time recovery (restore to any second in the last 35 days).
_MdxDesc.ld: Restores always create a new table. Adds ~20% to storage cost.

streamType.description:
#### Stream item changes to trigger functions or batch jobs in real time.

---

- `KEYS_ONLY`: Only key attributes of the changed item.
- `NEW_IMAGE`: The full item after the change.
- `OLD_IMAGE`: The full item before the change.
- `NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES`: Both before and after — useful for change tracking and auditing.

_MdxDesc.sd: Stream item changes to trigger functions or batch jobs in real time.
_MdxDesc.ld:
• KEYS_ONLY: Only key attributes of the changed item.
  • NEW_IMAGE: The full item after the change.
  • OLD_IMAGE: The full item before the change.
  • NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES: Both before and after — useful for change tracking and auditing.

secondaryIndexes.description:
#### Additional indexes for querying by attributes other than the primary key.

---

Without indexes, you can only query by primary key. Add a secondary index to query by
any attribute (e.g., query orders by `status` or users by `email`).

_MdxDesc.sd: Additional indexes for querying by attributes other than the primary key.
_MdxDesc.ld: Without indexes, you can only query by primary key. Add a secondary index to query by any attribute (e.g., query orders by status or users by email).

dev.description: #### Dev mode: runs locally in Docker by default. Set `remote: true` to use the deployed table.

_MdxDesc.sd: Dev mode: runs locally in Docker by default. Set remote: true to use the deployed table.

partitionKey.description: #### The main key attribute (e.g., `userId`, `orderId`). Must be unique if no sort key is used.

_MdxDesc.sd: The main key attribute (e.g., userId, orderId). Must be unique if no sort key is used.

sortKey.description:
#### Optional second key for composite keys. Enables range queries and multiple items per partition key.

---

E.g., partition key `userId` + sort key `createdAt` lets you query all items for a user sorted by date.

_MdxDesc.sd: Optional second key for composite keys. Enables range queries and multiple items per partition key.
_MdxDesc.ld: E.g., partition key userId + sort key createdAt lets you query all items for a user sorted by date.

_MdxDesc.sd: Attribute name (e.g., userId, email, createdAt).
Attribute data type: string, number, or binary.

readUnits.description:
#### Read capacity units per second. 1 unit = one 4 KB strongly consistent read (or two eventually consistent).

---

Requests exceeding this limit get throttled. Use `readScaling` to auto-adjust.

---

_MdxDesc.ld: Requests exceeding this limit get throttled. Use writeScaling to auto-adjust.

_MdxDesc.sd: Auto-scale write capacity based on actual usage. Scales up/down between min and max units.

readScaling.description: #### Auto-scale read capacity based on actual usage. Scales up/down between min and max units.

_MdxDesc.sd: Auto-scale read capacity based on actual usage. Scales up/down between min and max units.

_MdxDesc.sd: Minimum write units. Capacity never scales below this.

maxUnits.description: #### Maximum write units. Capacity never scales above this.

_MdxDesc.sd: Maximum write units. Capacity never scales above this.

keepUtilizationUnder.description: #### Target utilization percentage (e.g., 70). Scales up when usage exceeds this, down when it drops.

_MdxDesc.sd: Target utilization percentage (e.g., 70). Scales up when usage exceeds this, down when it drops.

minUnits.description: #### Minimum read units. Capacity never scales below this.

_MdxDesc.sd: Minimum read units. Capacity never scales below this.

maxUnits.description: #### Maximum read units. Capacity never scales above this.

_MdxDesc.sd: Maximum read units. Capacity never scales above this.

name.description: #### Name of the index (used when querying).

_MdxDesc.sd: Name of the index (used when querying).

partitionKey.description: #### Partition key for this index — the attribute you'll query by.

The table's primary key is always projected. List additional attributes you need in query results.

---

Define multi-step workflows with branching, retries, parallel execution, and error handling —
all without writing orchestration code. Pay per state transition (~$0.025/1,000 transitions).
Defined using [Amazon States Language (ASL)](https://states-language.net/spec.html).

```yaml
overrides:

    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

MongoDbAtlasCluster.description:

---

Document database with flexible schemas — great for content management, user profiles, catalogs, and apps
where your data model evolves. Starts at M2 (shared, ~$9/month) or M10 (dedicated, ~$57/month).

_MdxDesc.sd: Number of shards. More than 1 enables sharded mode for horizontal scaling. Requires M30+.

biConnector.description: #### BI Connector for SQL-based access to MongoDB data. CPU-intensive — may degrade M10/M20 performance.

_MdxDesc.sd: BI Connector for SQL-based access to MongoDB data. CPU-intensive — may degrade M10/M20 performance.

autoScaling.description:
#### Auto-scale tier and/or storage based on CPU/memory usage. Set min/max tier to control costs.

---

Scales up when CPU or memory exceeds 75% for 1 hour. Scales down when both are below 50% for 24 hours.

---

Not required for app-to-database access via `connectTo` — that's handled automatically.

numElectableNodes.description: #### Nodes that can become primary. More = better redundancy. Must be odd.

numReadOnlyNodes.description: #### Read-only replica nodes for scaling read throughput.

_MdxDesc.sd: Read-only replica nodes for scaling read throughput.

readPreference.description: #### Which node type the BI Connector reads from. Use `analytics` to avoid impacting production queries.
