enabled.description: #### Enable the BI Connector for SQL-based access.

_MdxDesc.sd: Enable the BI Connector for SQL-based access.

minClusterTier.description: #### Lowest tier the cluster can scale down to. Prevents unexpected cost increases from always scaling up.

_MdxDesc.sd: Lowest tier the cluster can scale down to. Prevents unexpected cost increases from always scaling up.

maxClusterTier.description: #### Highest tier the cluster can scale up to. Set a ceiling to control maximum costs.

_MdxDesc.sd: Highest tier the cluster can scale up to. Set a ceiling to control maximum costs.

disableDiskScaling.description: #### Prevent automatic disk expansion. By default, storage grows when usage hits 90%. Storage never scales down.

_MdxDesc.sd: Prevent automatic disk expansion. By default, storage grows when usage hits 90%. Storage never scales down.

disableScaleDown.description: #### Prevent automatic scale-down. The cluster can only scale up, never back down to a smaller tier.

_MdxDesc.sd: Prevent automatic scale-down. The cluster can only scale up, never back down to a smaller tier.
Name of the admin user
Password for the admin user

RedisCluster.description: #### In-memory data store for caching, sessions, queues, and real-time data. Sub-millisecond latency.

overrides.description:
#### Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.sd: In-memory data store for caching, sessions, queues, and real-time data. Sub-millisecond latency.

enableSharding.description:
#### Split data across multiple shards for horizontal scaling.

---

Each shard has its own primary + replicas. Routing is automatic.

> **Must be set at creation time** — can't be added later.
> Requires `numReplicaNodes >= 1`. Replica count can't be changed after creation.

_MdxDesc.sd: Split data across multiple shards for horizontal scaling.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Each shard has its own primary + replicas. Routing is automatic.

Must be set at creation time — can't be added later. Requires numReplicaNodes >= 1. Replica count can't be changed after creation.

_MdxDesc.sd: Number of shards (only with enableSharding: true).

numReplicaNodes.description:
#### Read replicas per shard. Increases read throughput and availability.

---

If the primary fails and `enableAutomaticFailover` is on, a replica takes over.
Can't be changed after creation for sharded clusters.

_MdxDesc.sd: Read replicas per shard. Increases read throughput and availability.
_MdxDesc.ld: If the primary fails and enableAutomaticFailover is on, a replica takes over. Can't be changed after creation for sharded clusters.

enableAutomaticFailover.description:
#### Auto-promote a replica to primary if the primary node fails.

---

Requires `numReplicaNodes >= 1`. Always enabled for sharded clusters.

> Deploy replicas first, then enable failover in a separate deployment.

_MdxDesc.sd: Auto-promote a replica to primary if the primary node fails.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Requires numReplicaNodes >= 1. Always enabled for sharded clusters.

Deploy replicas first, then enable failover in a separate deployment.

instanceSize.description:
#### The size of each Redis node. Affects memory, performance, and cost.

---

**Quick guide:**
- **`cache.t4g.micro`** (~$0.016/hr, 0.5 GB): Development, testing, low-traffic apps.
- **`cache.t4g.small`** (~$0.032/hr, 1.37 GB): Small production apps, session stores.
- **`cache.m7g.large`** (~$0.15/hr, 6.38 GB): Production workloads with moderate data.
- **`cache.r7g.large`** (~$0.20/hr, 13.07 GB): Large datasets, memory-heavy caching.

**Families:** `t` = burstable (cheap, variable). `m` = general purpose. `r` = memory-optimized.
Suffix `g` = ARM/Graviton (better price-performance).

This size applies to every node (primary + replicas). You can change it later without data loss.

_MdxDesc.sd: The size of each Redis node. Affects memory, performance, and cost.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Quick guide:

  • cache.t4g.micro (~$0.016/hr, 0.5 GB): Development, testing, low-traffic apps.
  • cache.t4g.small (~$0.032/hr, 1.37 GB): Small production apps, session stores.
  • cache.m7g.large (~$0.15/hr, 6.38 GB): Production workloads with moderate data.
  • cache.r7g.large (~$0.20/hr, 13.07 GB): Large datasets, memory-heavy caching.

Families: t = burstable (cheap, variable). m = general purpose. r = memory-optimized. Suffix g = ARM/Graviton (better price-performance).

This size applies to every node (primary + replicas). You can change it later without data loss.

accessibilityMode.description:

---

- **`vpc`** (default): Any resource in the same VPC (functions with `joinDefaultVpc: true`, containers, batch jobs).

Redis clusters don't have public IPs — you can't connect from your local machine directly.
Use a bastion host for local access.

connectTo.description:
#### Give this resource access to other resources in your stack.

---

List the names of resources this workload needs to communicate with. Stacktape automatically:
- **Grants IAM permissions** (e.g., S3 read/write, SQS send/receive)
- **Opens network access** (security group rules for databases, Redis)
- **Injects environment variables** with connection details: `STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[PARAM]`

Example: `connectTo: ["myDatabase", "myBucket"]` gives this workload full access to both
resources and injects `STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING`, `STP_MY_BUCKET_NAME`, etc.

---

#### What each resource type provides:

**`Bucket`** — read/write/delete objects → `NAME`, `ARN`

**`DynamoDbTable`** — CRUD + scan/query → `NAME`, `ARN`, `STREAM_ARN`

**`RelationalDatabase`** — network access + connection details → `CONNECTION_STRING`, `HOST`, `PORT`.
Aurora also gets `READER_CONNECTION_STRING`, `READER_HOST`.

**`MongoDbAtlasCluster`** — temporary credential-less access → `CONNECTION_STRING`

**`RedisCluster`** — network access → `HOST`, `READER_HOST`, `PORT`

**`Function`** — invoke permission → `ARN`

**`BatchJob`** — submit/list/terminate → `JOB_DEFINITION_ARN`, `STATE_MACHINE_ARN`

**`EventBus`** — publish events → `ARN`

**`UserAuthPool`** — full control → `ID`, `CLIENT_ID`, `ARN`

**`SqsQueue`** — send/receive/delete → `ARN`, `NAME`, `URL`

**`SnsTopic`** — publish/subscribe → `ARN`, `NAME`

**`UpstashRedis`** → `HOST`, `PORT`, `PASSWORD`, `REST_TOKEN`, `REST_URL`, `REDIS_URL`

**`PrivateService`** → `ADDRESS`

**`aws:ses`** — full SES email sending permissions

_MdxDesc.sd: Give this resource access to other resources in your stack.
_MdxDesc.ld:
List the names of resources this workload needs to communicate with. Stacktape automatically:

  • Grants IAM permissions (e.g., S3 read/write, SQS send/receive)
  • Opens network access (security group rules for databases, Redis)
  • Injects environment variables with connection details: STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[PARAM]

Example: connectTo: ["myDatabase", "myBucket"] gives this workload full access to both resources and injects STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING, STP_MY_BUCKET_NAME, etc.

iamRoleStatements.description:
#### Raw IAM policy statements for permissions not covered by `connectTo`.

---

Added as a separate policy alongside auto-generated permissions. Use this for
accessing AWS services directly (e.g., Rekognition, Textract, Bedrock).

_MdxDesc.sd: Raw IAM policy statements for permissions not covered by connectTo.
_MdxDesc.ld: Added as a separate policy alongside auto-generated permissions. Use this for accessing AWS services directly (e.g., Rekognition, Textract, Bedrock).

StpBuildpackLambdaPackaging.description:
#### A zero-config buildpack that packages your code for AWS Lambda.

---

The `stacktape-lambda-buildpack` automatically bundles your code and dependencies into an optimized Lambda deployment package.

**Supported languages:** JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, Go, Ruby, PHP, and .NET.

For JS/TS, your code is bundled into a single file. Source maps are automatically generated.
Packages are cached based on a checksum, so unchanged code is not re-packaged.

_MdxDesc.sd: A zero-config buildpack that packages your code for AWS Lambda.
_MdxDesc.ld:
The stacktape-lambda-buildpack automatically bundles your code and dependencies into an optimized Lambda deployment package.

Supported languages: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, Go, Ruby, PHP, and .NET.

For JS/TS, your code is bundled into a single file. Source maps are automatically generated. Packages are cached based on a checksum, so unchanged code is not re-packaged.

handlerFunction.description: #### The name of the handler function to be executed when the Lambda is invoked.

_MdxDesc.sd: The name of the handler function to be executed when the Lambda is invoked.

entryfilePath.description:
#### Path to your app's entry point, relative to the Stacktape config file.

---

For JS/TS: code is bundled into a single file. Dependencies with native binaries are installed separately.
For Python: use `module/file.py:app` format when using `runAppAs` (WSGI/ASGI).

_MdxDesc.sd: Path to your app's entry point, relative to the Stacktape config file.
_MdxDesc.ld: For JS/TS: code is bundled into a single file. Dependencies with native binaries are installed separately. For Python: use module/file.py:app format when using runAppAs (WSGI/ASGI).

---

_MdxDesc.ld: The path is relative to your Stacktape configuration file.

---

excludeDependencies.description: #### A list of dependencies to exclude from the deployment package.

Language-specific packaging configuration.

CustomArtifactLambdaPackaging.description:
#### Uses a pre-built artifact for Lambda deployment.

---

With `custom-artifact`, you provide a path to your own pre-built deployment package.
If the specified path is a directory or an unzipped file, Stacktape will automatically zip it.

This is useful when you have custom build processes or need full control over the packaging.

_MdxDesc.ld:

This is useful when you have custom build processes or need full control over the packaging.

#### Serverless Redis by Upstash — pay-per-request with no idle costs.

---

Perfect for Lambda-based apps where a traditional Redis cluster would be wasteful.
Accessible over HTTPS (REST API) or standard Redis protocol. Great for caching, sessions, rate limiting.

#### Embed an AWS CDK construct directly in your Stacktape stack.

---

Escape hatch for resources not natively supported by Stacktape. Write a CDK construct class
in TypeScript/JavaScript and Stacktape will synthesize and deploy it as part of your stack.

entryfilePath.description:
#### Path to the file containing your CDK construct class.

---

Supports `.js` and `.ts` files. The file must export a class that extends `Construct` from `aws-cdk-lib`.

_MdxDesc.ld: Supports .js and .ts files. The file must export a class that extends Construct from aws-cdk-lib.

---

Must match the exact export name. Use this when the file has multiple exports or uses named exports.

---

This object is forwarded as the third argument (`props`) to your construct class. Use `$ResourceParam()` and `$Secret()`
directives here to pass dynamic values from other resources in your stack.

SqsQueue.description:
#### Message queue for decoupling services. Producers send messages, consumers process them at their own pace.

---

Fully managed, serverless, pay-per-message. Use for background processing, task queues, or buffering between services.

overrides.description:
#### Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.sd: Message queue for decoupling services. Producers send messages, consumers process them at their own pace.
_MdxDesc.ld: Fully managed, serverless, pay-per-message. Use for background processing, task queues, or buffering between services.

delayMessagesSecond.description:
#### Delay (in seconds) before new messages become visible to consumers. Range: 0–900.

---

Useful for introducing a buffer, e.g., waiting for related data to be available before processing.

_MdxDesc.sd: Delay (in seconds) before new messages become visible to consumers. Range: 0–900.
_MdxDesc.ld: Useful for introducing a buffer, e.g., waiting for related data to be available before processing.

maxMessageSizeBytes.description:
#### Maximum message size in bytes. Range: 1,024 (1 KB) to 262,144 (256 KB).

---

Messages larger than this limit are rejected. For payloads over 256 KB, store the data in S3 and send the reference.

_MdxDesc.sd: Maximum message size in bytes. Range: 1,024 (1 KB) to 262,144 (256 KB).
_MdxDesc.ld: Messages larger than this limit are rejected. For payloads over 256 KB, store the data in S3 and send the reference.

messageRetentionPeriodSeconds.description:
#### How long unprocessed messages stay in the queue before being deleted. Range: 60s to 1,209,600s (14 days).

---

Default is 4 days (345,600s). Increase if consumers might fall behind or be temporarily offline.

_MdxDesc.sd: How long unprocessed messages stay in the queue before being deleted. Range: 60s to 1,209,600s (14 days).
_MdxDesc.ld: Default is 4 days (345,600s). Increase if consumers might fall behind or be temporarily offline.

longPollingSeconds.description:
#### Seconds the queue waits for messages before returning an empty response. Range: 0–20.

---

Set to `1`–`20` to enable long polling, which reduces costs by making fewer API calls.
With short polling (`0`), the consumer gets an immediate (often empty) response and must poll again.

Recommended: `20` for most workloads — it's the most cost-effective.

_MdxDesc.sd: Seconds the queue waits for messages before returning an empty response. Range: 0–20.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Set to 1–20 to enable long polling, which reduces costs by making fewer API calls. With short polling (0), the consumer gets an immediate (often empty) response and must poll again.

Recommended: 20 for most workloads — it's the most cost-effective.

visibilityTimeoutSeconds.description:
#### How long (seconds) a message is hidden from other consumers after being received. Range: 0–43,200 (12 hours).

---

After a consumer picks up a message, it must delete it before this timeout expires — otherwise it becomes
visible again and can be processed by another consumer (duplicate processing).

Set this higher than your expected processing time. If your tasks take 2 minutes, use at least 150 seconds.

_MdxDesc.sd: How long (seconds) a message is hidden from other consumers after being received. Range: 0–43,200 (12 hours).
_MdxDesc.ld:
After a consumer picks up a message, it must delete it before this timeout expires — otherwise it becomes visible again and can be processed by another consumer (duplicate processing).

Set this higher than your expected processing time. If your tasks take 2 minutes, use at least 150 seconds.

fifoEnabled.description:
#### Creates a FIFO queue that guarantees message order and exactly-once delivery.

---

Use when processing order matters (e.g., financial transactions, sequential workflows).
FIFO queues have lower throughput (~300 msg/s without batching, ~3,000 with) compared to standard queues.

Requires either `contentBasedDeduplication: true` or a `MessageDeduplicationId` on each message.

_MdxDesc.sd: Creates a FIFO queue that guarantees message order and exactly-once delivery.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use when processing order matters (e.g., financial transactions, sequential workflows). FIFO queues have lower throughput (~300 msg/s without batching, ~3,000 with) compared to standard queues.

Requires either contentBasedDeduplication: true or a MessageDeduplicationId on each message.

fifoHighThroughput.description:
#### Enables high-throughput mode for FIFO queues (up to ~70,000 msg/s per queue).

---

Messages are partitioned by `MessageGroupId` — order is guaranteed within each group but not across groups.
Requires `fifoEnabled: true`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Enables high-throughput mode for FIFO queues (up to ~70,000 msg/s per queue).
_MdxDesc.ld: Messages are partitioned by MessageGroupId — order is guaranteed within each group but not across groups. Requires fifoEnabled: true.

contentBasedDeduplication.description:
#### Automatically deduplicates messages based on their content (SHA-256 hash of the body).

---

Within the 5-minute deduplication window, identical messages are delivered only once.
Saves you from having to generate a unique `MessageDeduplicationId` for each message.
Requires `fifoEnabled: true`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Automatically deduplicates messages based on their content (SHA-256 hash of the body).
_MdxDesc.ld: Within the 5-minute deduplication window, identical messages are delivered only once. Saves you from having to generate a unique MessageDeduplicationId for each message. Requires fifoEnabled: true.

redrivePolicy.description:
#### Moves messages that fail processing too many times to a dead-letter queue for inspection.

---

After `maxReceiveCount` failed attempts, the message is automatically moved to a separate queue
so you can investigate and reprocess it. Prevents poison messages from blocking the queue.

_MdxDesc.sd: Moves messages that fail processing too many times to a dead-letter queue for inspection.
_MdxDesc.ld: After maxReceiveCount failed attempts, the message is automatically moved to a separate queue so you can investigate and reprocess it. Prevents poison messages from blocking the queue.

alarms.description:
#### Additional alarms associated with this resource.

---

These alarms will be merged with any alarms configured globally in the [console](https://console.stacktape.com/alarms).

_MdxDesc.sd: Additional alarms associated with this resource.
_MdxDesc.ld: These alarms will be merged with any alarms configured globally in the console.

disabledGlobalAlarms.description:
#### Disables globally configured alarms for this resource.

---

Provide a list of alarm names as configured in the [console](https://console.stacktape.com/alarms).

_MdxDesc.sd: Disables globally configured alarms for this resource.
_MdxDesc.ld: Provide a list of alarm names as configured in the console.

policyStatements.description:
#### Custom access-control statements added to the queue's resource policy.

---

These are merged with policies Stacktape auto-generates. Use to grant cross-account access or allow
specific AWS services (e.g., SNS) to send messages to this queue.

_MdxDesc.sd: Custom access-control statements added to the queue's resource policy.
_MdxDesc.ld: These are merged with policies Stacktape auto-generates. Use to grant cross-account access or allow specific AWS services (e.g., SNS) to send messages to this queue.

events.description:
#### A list of event sources that trigger message delivery to this queue.

---

Currently supports EventBridge event bus integration for delivering events directly to the queue.

_MdxDesc.sd: A list of event sources that trigger message delivery to this queue.
_MdxDesc.ld: Currently supports EventBridge event bus integration for delivering events directly to the queue.

targetSqsQueueName.description: #### Name of another `sqs-queue` in your config to use as the dead-letter queue.

_MdxDesc.sd: Name of another sqs-queue in your config to use as the dead-letter queue.

targetSqsQueueArn.description: #### ARN of an external SQS queue to use as the dead-letter queue. Use when the DLQ is in another stack or account.

_MdxDesc.sd: ARN of an external SQS queue to use as the dead-letter queue. Use when the DLQ is in another stack or account.

maxReceiveCount.description:
#### How many times a message can be received (and fail) before being moved to the dead-letter queue.

---

A typical starting value is `3`–`5`. Set lower for fast-failing workloads, higher for retryable transient errors.

_MdxDesc.sd: How many times a message can be received (and fail) before being moved to the dead-letter queue.
_MdxDesc.ld: A typical starting value is 3–5. Set lower for fast-failing workloads, higher for retryable transient errors.

evaluation.description:
#### How long and how often to evaluate the metric before triggering.

---

Controls the evaluation window (period), how many periods to look at, and how many must breach
the threshold to fire the alarm. Useful for filtering out short spikes.

_MdxDesc.sd: How long and how often to evaluate the metric before triggering.
_MdxDesc.ld: Controls the evaluation window (period), how many periods to look at, and how many must breach the threshold to fire the alarm. Useful for filtering out short spikes.

notificationTargets.description: #### Where to send notifications when the alarm fires — Slack, MS Teams, or email.

_MdxDesc.sd: Where to send notifications when the alarm fires — Slack, MS Teams, or email.

includeInHistory.description: #### Whether alarm state changes should appear in monitoring history.

_MdxDesc.sd: Whether alarm state changes should appear in monitoring history.

description.description: #### Custom alarm description used in notification messages and the AWS console.

_MdxDesc.sd: Custom alarm description used in notification messages and the AWS console.

thresholdCount.description:
#### Fires when received message count crosses this threshold.

---

Default: fires if **average** messages received per period > `thresholdCount`.
Customize with `statistic` and `comparisonOperator`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Fires when received message count crosses this threshold.
_MdxDesc.ld: Default: fires if average messages received per period > thresholdCount. Customize with statistic and comparisonOperator.

comparisonOperator.description: #### How to compare the metric value against the threshold.

_MdxDesc.sd: How to compare the metric value against the threshold.

statistic.description: #### How to aggregate metric values within each period: `avg`, `sum`, `min`, `max`, `p90`, `p95`, `p99`.

_MdxDesc.sd: How to aggregate metric values within each period: avg, sum, min, max, p90, p95, p99.

type.description:
#### Fires when the SQS queue has unprocessed messages.

---

The queue is considered "not empty" if any of these are non-zero: visible messages,
in-flight messages, messages received, or messages sent.

_MdxDesc.sd: Fires when the SQS queue has unprocessed messages.
_MdxDesc.ld: The queue is considered "not empty" if any of these are non-zero: visible messages, in-flight messages, messages received, or messages sent.

_MdxDesc.sd: Allow or Deny access for the specified actions and principal.

SqsQueueEventBusIntegration.description: #### Routes events from an EventBridge event bus to this queue when they match a specified pattern.

_MdxDesc.sd: Properties of the integration
Routes events from an EventBridge event bus to this queue when they match a specified pattern.

messageGroupId.description:
#### Message group ID for FIFO queues. Required when the target queue has `fifoEnabled: true`.

---

Messages in the same group are processed in strict order. Different groups can be processed in parallel.

_MdxDesc.sd: Message group ID for FIFO queues. Required when the target queue has fifoEnabled: true.
_MdxDesc.ld: Messages in the same group are processed in strict order. Different groups can be processed in parallel.

eventBusArn.description:
#### The ARN of an existing event bus.

---

Use this to subscribe to an event bus that is not managed by your stack.
You must specify only one of `eventBusArn`, `eventBusName`, or `useDefaultBus`.

_MdxDesc.sd: The ARN of an existing event bus.
_MdxDesc.ld: Use this to subscribe to an event bus that is not managed by your stack. You must specify only one of eventBusArn, eventBusName, or useDefaultBus.

eventBusName.description:
#### The name of an event bus defined in your stack's resources.

---

You must specify only one of `eventBusArn`, `eventBusName`, or `useDefaultBus`.

_MdxDesc.sd: The name of an event bus defined in your stack's resources.
_MdxDesc.ld: You must specify only one of eventBusArn, eventBusName, or useDefaultBus.

useDefaultBus.description:
#### Uses the default AWS event bus.

---

You must specify only one of `eventBusArn`, `eventBusName`, or `useDefaultBus`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Uses the default AWS event bus.
_MdxDesc.ld: You must specify only one of eventBusArn, eventBusName, or useDefaultBus.

eventPattern.description:
#### A pattern to filter events from the event bus.

---

Only events that match this pattern will trigger the target.
For details on the syntax, see the [AWS EventBridge documentation on event patterns](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eventbridge/latest/userguide/eb-event-patterns.html).

_MdxDesc.sd: A pattern to filter events from the event bus.
_MdxDesc.ld: Only events that match this pattern will trigger the target. For details on the syntax, see the AWS EventBridge documentation on event patterns.

onDeliveryFailure.description:
#### A destination for events that fail to be delivered to the target.

---

In rare cases, an event might fail to be delivered. This property specifies an SQS queue where failed events will be sent.

_MdxDesc.sd: A destination for events that fail to be delivered to the target.
_MdxDesc.ld: In rare cases, an event might fail to be delivered. This property specifies an SQS queue where failed events will be sent.

input.description:
#### A fixed JSON object to be passed as the event payload.

---

If you need to customize the payload based on the event, use `inputTransformer` instead.
You can only use one of `input`, `inputPath`, or `inputTransformer`.

Example:

```yaml
input:
  source: 'my-custom-event'
```

inputPath.description:
#### A JSONPath expression to extract a portion of the event to pass to the target.

---

This is useful for forwarding only a specific part of the event payload.
You can only use one of `input`, `inputPath`, or `inputTransformer`.

Example:

```yaml
inputPath: '$.detail'
```

_MdxDesc.sd: A JSONPath expression to extract a portion of the event to pass to the target.
_MdxDesc.ld:
This is useful for forwarding only a specific part of the event payload. You can only use one of input, inputPath, or inputTransformer.

Example:

inputPath: '$.detail'

inputTransformer.description:
#### Customizes the event payload sent to the target.

---

This allows you to extract values from the original event and use them to construct a new payload.
You can only use one of `input`, `inputPath`, or `inputTransformer`.

Example:

```yaml
inputTransformer:
  inputPathsMap:
    instanceId: '$.detail.instance-id'
    instanceState: '$.detail.state'
  inputTemplate:
    message: 'Instance <instanceId> is now in state <instanceState>.'
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Customizes the event payload sent to the target.
_MdxDesc.ld:
This allows you to extract values from the original event and use them to construct a new payload. You can only use one of input, inputPath, or inputTransformer.

Example:

inputTransformer:
  inputPathsMap:
    instanceId: '$.detail.instance-id'
    instanceState: '$.detail.state'
  inputTemplate:
    message: 'Instance <instanceId> is now in state <instanceState>.'

SnsTopic.description:
#### Pub/sub messaging: publish once, deliver to many subscribers (Lambda, SQS, email, SMS, HTTP).

---

Serverless, pay-per-message. Use when one event needs to trigger multiple actions — e.g., order placed
triggers email confirmation + inventory update + analytics. Subscribers are configured on the subscriber side.

overrides.description:
#### Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.sd: Pub/sub messaging: publish once, deliver to many subscribers (Lambda, SQS, email, SMS, HTTP).
_MdxDesc.ld: Serverless, pay-per-message. Use when one event needs to trigger multiple actions — e.g., order placed triggers email confirmation + inventory update + analytics. Subscribers are configured on the subscriber side.

smsDisplayName.description: #### Sender name shown on SMS messages sent to subscribers (e.g., "MyApp"). Max 11 characters.

_MdxDesc.sd: Sender name shown on SMS messages sent to subscribers (e.g., "MyApp"). Max 11 characters.

fifoEnabled.description:
#### Guarantees message order and exactly-once delivery. Use for financial transactions, sequential workflows.

---

FIFO topics can only deliver to FIFO SQS queues (not email, SMS, or HTTP).
Requires either `contentBasedDeduplication: true` or a unique `MessageDeduplicationId` per message.

_MdxDesc.sd: Guarantees message order and exactly-once delivery. Use for financial transactions, sequential workflows.
_MdxDesc.ld: FIFO topics can only deliver to FIFO SQS queues (not email, SMS, or HTTP). Requires either contentBasedDeduplication: true or a unique MessageDeduplicationId per message.

contentBasedDeduplication.description:
#### Automatically deduplicates messages based on content (SHA-256 hash) within a 5-minute window.

---

Saves you from generating a unique deduplication ID for each message. Requires `fifoEnabled: true`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Automatically deduplicates messages based on content (SHA-256 hash) within a 5-minute window.
_MdxDesc.ld: Saves you from generating a unique deduplication ID for each message. Requires fifoEnabled: true.

#### Host a static website (React, Vue, Astro, etc.) on S3 + CloudFront CDN.

---

Combines S3 storage with a global CDN for fast, cheap, and scalable static site hosting.
Includes build step, custom domains, caching presets, and environment injection.

overrides:

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

overrides:

    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.sd: Host a static website (React, Vue, Astro, etc.) on S3 + CloudFront CDN.
_MdxDesc.ld: Combines S3 storage with a global CDN for fast, cheap, and scalable static site hosting. Includes build step, custom domains, caching presets, and environment injection.

This folder's contents are uploaded to the bucket on every deploy.

Runs during the packaging phase, in parallel with other resources. Bundle size is shown in deploy logs.

_MdxDesc.ld: Runs during the packaging phase, in parallel with other resources. Bundle size is shown in deploy logs.

_MdxDesc.ld: Used by stacktape dev.

excludeFilesPatterns.description: #### Glob patterns for files to skip during upload (relative to `uploadDirectoryPath`).

hostingContentType.description:
#### Optimizes caching and routing for your type of frontend app.

---

- **`single-page-app`**: For React, Vue, Angular, or any SPA built with Vite/Webpack.
  Enables client-side routing (e.g., `/about` serves `index.html`). HTML is never browser-cached;
  hashed assets (`.js`, `.css`) are cached forever.

- **`static-website`** (default): For multi-page static sites. All files are CDN-cached
  but never browser-cached, so users always see the latest content after a deploy.

- **`astro-static-website`** / **`sveltekit-static-website`** / **`nuxt-static-website`**:
  Framework-specific presets that cache hashed build assets (`_astro/`, `_app/`, `_nuxt/`)
  indefinitely while keeping HTML fresh.

- **`gatsby-static-website`**: Gatsby-specific caching following their recommendations.

You can override any preset's behavior using `fileOptions`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Optimizes caching and routing for your type of frontend app.
_MdxDesc.ld:
• single-page-app: For React, Vue, Angular, or any SPA built with Vite/Webpack. Enables client-side routing (e.g., /about serves index.html). HTML is never browser-cached; hashed assets (.js, .css) are cached forever.

  • static-website (default): For multi-page static sites. All files are CDN-cached but never browser-cached, so users always see the latest content after a deploy.

  • astro-static-website / sveltekit-static-website / nuxt-static-website: Framework-specific presets that cache hashed build assets (_astro/, _app/, _nuxt/) indefinitely while keeping HTML fresh.

  • gatsby-static-website: Gatsby-specific caching following their recommendations.

You can override any preset's behavior using fileOptions.

customDomains.description:
#### Custom domains (e.g., `www.example.com`). Stacktape auto-creates DNS records and TLS certificates.

---

Your domain must be added as a Route53 hosted zone in your AWS account first.

_MdxDesc.ld: Your domain must be added as a Route53 hosted zone in your AWS account first.

connectTo.description:
#### Give this resource access to other resources in your stack.

---

List the names of resources this workload needs to communicate with. Stacktape automatically:
- **Grants IAM permissions** (e.g., S3 read/write, SQS send/receive)
- **Opens network access** (security group rules for databases, Redis)
- **Injects environment variables** with connection details: `STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[PARAM]`

Example: `connectTo: ["myDatabase", "myBucket"]` gives this workload full access to both
resources and injects `STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING`, `STP_MY_BUCKET_NAME`, etc.

---

#### What each resource type provides:

**`Bucket`** — read/write/delete objects → `NAME`, `ARN`

**`DynamoDbTable`** — CRUD + scan/query → `NAME`, `ARN`, `STREAM_ARN`

**`RelationalDatabase`** — network access + connection details → `CONNECTION_STRING`, `HOST`, `PORT`.
Aurora also gets `READER_CONNECTION_STRING`, `READER_HOST`.

**`MongoDbAtlasCluster`** — temporary credential-less access → `CONNECTION_STRING`

**`RedisCluster`** — network access → `HOST`, `READER_HOST`, `PORT`

**`Function`** — invoke permission → `ARN`

**`BatchJob`** — submit/list/terminate → `JOB_DEFINITION_ARN`, `STATE_MACHINE_ARN`

**`EventBus`** — publish events → `ARN`

**`UserAuthPool`** — full control → `ID`, `CLIENT_ID`, `ARN`

**`SqsQueue`** — send/receive/delete → `ARN`, `NAME`, `URL`

**`SnsTopic`** — publish/subscribe → `ARN`, `NAME`

**`UpstashRedis`** → `HOST`, `PORT`, `PASSWORD`, `REST_TOKEN`, `REST_URL`, `REDIS_URL`

**`PrivateService`** → `ADDRESS`

**`aws:ses`** — full SES email sending permissions

_MdxDesc.sd: Give this resource access to other resources in your stack.
_MdxDesc.ld:
List the names of resources this workload needs to communicate with. Stacktape automatically:

  • Grants IAM permissions (e.g., S3 read/write, SQS send/receive)
  • Opens network access (security group rules for databases, Redis)
  • Injects environment variables with connection details: STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[PARAM]

Example: connectTo: ["myDatabase", "myBucket"] gives this workload full access to both resources and injects STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING, STP_MY_BUCKET_NAME, etc.

iamRoleStatements.description:
#### Raw IAM policy statements for permissions not covered by `connectTo`.

---

Added as a separate policy alongside auto-generated permissions. Use this for
accessing AWS services directly (e.g., Rekognition, Textract, Bedrock).

_MdxDesc.sd: Raw IAM policy statements for permissions not covered by connectTo.
_MdxDesc.ld: Added as a separate policy alongside auto-generated permissions. Use this for accessing AWS services directly (e.g., Rekognition, Textract, Bedrock).

memory.description: #### Memory in MB (128–10,240). CPU scales proportionally — 1,769 MB = 1 vCPU.

_MdxDesc.sd: Memory in MB (128–10,240). CPU scales proportionally — 1,769 MB = 1 vCPU.

#### Connect to VPC resources (databases, Redis). **Warning:** function loses direct internet access.

---

Uses Kinesis Data Firehose (~$0.03/GB). Failed deliveries go to a backup S3 bucket.

disableInvalidationAfterDeploy.description:
#### Skip clearing the CDN cache after each deploy.

---

By default, all cached content is flushed on every deploy so users see the latest version.
Set to `true` if you manage cache invalidation yourself or want to keep cached content between deploys.

defaultCachingOptions.description:

Useful when you want to cache SSR responses longer than the framework defaults.

---

Matches existing framework-managed paths (e.g. `_astro/*`, `_next/data/*`) or adds
new server-path caching rules (e.g. `/_server-islands/*`) while preserving managed routing.

_MdxDesc.ld: Matches existing framework-managed paths (e.g. _astro/*, _next/data/*) or adds new server-path caching rules (e.g. /_server-islands/*) while preserving managed routing.

OpenSearchDomain.description:
#### Managed search and analytics engine (OpenSearch/Elasticsearch compatible).

---

Full-text search, log analytics, and real-time dashboards. Use for search features in your app,
centralized logging, or time-series data analysis. Costs start at ~$50/month (single small node).

#### Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.sd: Managed search and analytics engine (OpenSearch/Elasticsearch compatible).
_MdxDesc.ld: Full-text search, log analytics, and real-time dashboards. Use for search features in your app, centralized logging, or time-series data analysis. Costs start at ~$50/month (single small node).

version.description: #### OpenSearch engine version. Pin this to avoid surprises when the default changes.

_MdxDesc.sd: OpenSearch engine version. Pin this to avoid surprises when the default changes.

clusterConfig.description:
#### Instance types, counts, and cluster topology (data nodes, master nodes, warm storage).

---

Defaults to a single `m4.large.search` node if not specified.

_MdxDesc.ld: Defaults to a single m4.large.search node if not specified.

storage.description:
#### EBS volume size, IOPS, and throughput per data node. Only for EBS-backed instance types.

---

`iops` and `throughput` settings only apply to GP3 volumes.

_MdxDesc.sd: EBS volume size, IOPS, and throughput per data node. Only for EBS-backed instance types.
_MdxDesc.ld: iops and throughput settings only apply to GP3 volumes.

userPool.description: #### Name of a `user-pool` resource in your config. Enables login to OpenSearch Dashboards via Cognito.

_MdxDesc.sd: Error logs, search slow logs, and indexing slow logs. Sent to CloudWatch automatically.

accessibility.description:
#### Network access mode: public internet (default), VPC-only, or VPC with security-group scoping.

---

Even in `internet` mode, access requires IAM credentials. VPC modes add network-level isolation.

instanceType.description:
#### Instance type for data nodes (e.g., `t3.medium.search`, `r6g.large.search`).

---

Data nodes store data and handle queries. For production, pair with dedicated master nodes.

_MdxDesc.sd: Instance type for data nodes (e.g., t3.medium.search, r6g.large.search).
_MdxDesc.ld: Data nodes store data and handle queries. For production, pair with dedicated master nodes.

instanceCount.description: #### Number of data nodes. More nodes = more storage capacity and query throughput.

_MdxDesc.sd: Number of data nodes. More nodes = more storage capacity and query throughput.

dedicatedMasterType.description:
#### Instance type for dedicated master nodes (e.g., `m5.large.search`). Manages cluster state, not data.

---

Recommended for clusters with 3+ data nodes to prevent split-brain. Use an odd count (3, 5, or 7).

_MdxDesc.sd: Instance type for dedicated master nodes (e.g., m5.large.search). Manages cluster state, not data.
_MdxDesc.ld: Recommended for clusters with 3+ data nodes to prevent split-brain. Use an odd count (3, 5, or 7).

_MdxDesc.sd: Number of dedicated master nodes. Must be odd (3, 5, or 7) for quorum.

warmType.description:
#### Instance type for warm (UltraWarm) nodes — cheaper storage for infrequently accessed data.

---

Data on warm nodes is still searchable but with higher query latency. Great for retaining old logs
or time-series data at lower cost.

_MdxDesc.sd: Instance type for warm (UltraWarm) nodes — cheaper storage for infrequently accessed data.
_MdxDesc.ld: Data on warm nodes is still searchable but with higher query latency. Great for retaining old logs or time-series data at lower cost.

warmCount.description: #### Number of warm (UltraWarm) nodes for lower-cost storage of older data.

_MdxDesc.sd: Number of warm (UltraWarm) nodes for lower-cost storage of older data.

multiAzDisabled.description:
#### Disable Multi-AZ replication. Not recommended — reduces availability and data durability.

---

Multi-AZ is auto-enabled for clusters with 2+ nodes. It distributes nodes across availability zones
so the cluster survives an AZ outage.

_MdxDesc.sd: Disable Multi-AZ replication. Not recommended — reduces availability and data durability.
_MdxDesc.ld: Multi-AZ is auto-enabled for clusters with 2+ nodes. It distributes nodes across availability zones so the cluster survives an AZ outage.

#### Enable Multi-AZ with a standby AZ for highest availability (99.99% SLA).

---

Distributes nodes across 3 AZs with one as standby. The standby takes over instantly during failures
without re-balancing. Requires: version 1.3+, 3 dedicated master + data nodes, GP3/SSD instances.

_MdxDesc.ld: Distributes nodes across 3 AZs with one as standby. The standby takes over instantly during failures without re-balancing. Requires: version 1.3+, 3 dedicated master + data nodes, GP3/SSD instances.

accessibilityMode.description:
#### How the domain can be reached.

---

- **`internet`**: Accessible from anywhere (still requires IAM credentials).
- **`vpc`**: Only accessible from resources inside your VPC (functions with `joinDefaultVpc: true`, containers, batch jobs).
- **`scoping-workloads-in-vpc`**: Like `vpc`, but also requires security-group access via `connectTo`.

**Cannot be changed after creation** — switching between internet and VPC modes requires a new domain.

_MdxDesc.sd: How the domain can be reached.
_MdxDesc.ld:
• internet: Accessible from anywhere (still requires IAM credentials).
  • vpc: Only accessible from resources inside your VPC (functions with joinDefaultVpc: true, containers, batch jobs).
  • scoping-workloads-in-vpc: Like vpc, but also requires security-group access via connectTo.

Cannot be changed after creation — switching between internet and VPC modes requires a new domain.

EfsFilesystem.description:
#### Shared file storage that multiple containers can read/write simultaneously.

---

Persistent, elastic (grows/shrinks automatically), and accessible from any container in your stack
via `volumeMounts`. Use for shared uploads, CMS media, ML model files, or anything that needs to
survive container restarts. Pay only for storage used (~$0.30/GB/month for standard access).

overrides.description:
#### Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.sd: Shared file storage that multiple containers can read/write simultaneously.
_MdxDesc.ld: Persistent, elastic (grows/shrinks automatically), and accessible from any container in your stack via volumeMounts. Use for shared uploads, CMS media, ML model files, or anything that needs to survive container restarts. Pay only for storage used (~$0.30/GB/month for standard access).

backupEnabled.description: #### Enable daily automatic backups with 35-day retention. Incremental (only changes are copied).

_MdxDesc.sd: Enable daily automatic backups with 35-day retention. Incremental (only changes are copied).

throughputMode.description:
#### How throughput scales with your workload.

---

- **`elastic`** (recommended): Auto-scales throughput. Best for spiky workloads (web apps, CI/CD).
- **`provisioned`**: Fixed throughput you set via `provisionedThroughputInMibps`. Best for steady high-throughput workloads.
- **`bursting`**: Throughput scales with storage size (50 KiB/s per GiB). Can run out of burst credits.

_MdxDesc.sd: How throughput scales with your workload.
_MdxDesc.ld:
• elastic (recommended): Auto-scales throughput. Best for spiky workloads (web apps, CI/CD).
  • provisioned: Fixed throughput you set via provisionedThroughputInMibps. Best for steady high-throughput workloads.
  • bursting: Throughput scales with storage size (50 KiB/s per GiB). Can run out of burst credits.

provisionedThroughputInMibps.description:
#### Guaranteed throughput in MiB/s. Required when `throughputMode` is `provisioned`.

---

E.g., `100` = 100 MiB/s. Additional fees apply based on the provisioned amount. Can be changed anytime.

_MdxDesc.sd: Guaranteed throughput in MiB/s. Required when throughputMode is provisioned.
_MdxDesc.ld: E.g., 100 = 100 MiB/s. Additional fees apply based on the provisioned amount. Can be changed anytime.

KinesisStream.description:
#### Real-time data stream for ingesting high-volume events (logs, clickstreams, IoT, analytics).

---

Continuously captures data from many producers. Consumers (Lambda functions, etc.) process records in order.
Use when you need real-time processing with sub-second latency, not just async messaging (use SQS for that).

overrides.description:
#### Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.sd: Real-time data stream for ingesting high-volume events (logs, clickstreams, IoT, analytics).
_MdxDesc.ld: Continuously captures data from many producers. Consumers (Lambda functions, etc.) process records in order. Use when you need real-time processing with sub-second latency, not just async messaging (use SQS for that).

capacityMode.description:
#### How the stream scales.

---

- **`ON_DEMAND`**: Auto-scales, pay per GB. Recommended for most use cases.
- **`PROVISIONED`**: You choose a fixed number of shards (1 MB/s write, 2 MB/s read each). More predictable costs.

_MdxDesc.sd: How the stream scales.
_MdxDesc.ld:
• ON_DEMAND: Auto-scales, pay per GB. Recommended for most use cases.
  • PROVISIONED: You choose a fixed number of shards (1 MB/s write, 2 MB/s read each). More predictable costs.

shardCount.description:
#### Number of shards. Only used when `capacityMode` is `PROVISIONED`.

---

Each shard: 1 MB/s write (1,000 records/s), 2 MB/s read (shared across consumers).

_MdxDesc.sd: Number of shards. Only used when capacityMode is PROVISIONED.
_MdxDesc.ld: Each shard: 1 MB/s write (1,000 records/s), 2 MB/s read (shared across consumers).

retentionPeriodHours.description: #### How long records stay in the stream (hours). Range: 24–8,760 (365 days). Beyond 24h costs extra.

_MdxDesc.sd: How long records stay in the stream (hours). Range: 24–8,760 (365 days). Beyond 24h costs extra.

encryption.description: #### Encrypt data at rest using a KMS key.

_MdxDesc.sd: Encrypt data at rest using a KMS key.

enableEnhancedFanOut.description:
#### Give each consumer its own dedicated 2 MB/s read throughput (instead of sharing).

---

Use when you have multiple consumers reading from the same stream and need low latency.
Enhanced fan-out consumers are auto-created when a Lambda uses `autoCreateConsumer: true`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Give each consumer its own dedicated 2 MB/s read throughput (instead of sharing).
_MdxDesc.ld: Use when you have multiple consumers reading from the same stream and need low latency. Enhanced fan-out consumers are auto-created when a Lambda uses autoCreateConsumer: true.

_MdxDesc.sd: Enable server-side encryption.

kmsKeyArn.description: #### ARN of your own KMS key. If omitted, uses the AWS-managed `alias/aws/kinesis` key (no extra cost).

_MdxDesc.sd: ARN of your own KMS key. If omitted, uses the AWS-managed alias/aws/kinesis key (no extra cost).

LambdaFunction.description:
#### A serverless compute resource that runs your code in response to events.

---

Lambda functions are short-lived, stateless, and scale automatically. You only pay for the compute time you consume.

overrides.description:
#### Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.sd: A serverless compute resource that runs your code in response to events.
_MdxDesc.ld: Lambda functions are short-lived, stateless, and scale automatically. You only pay for the compute time you consume.

packaging.description:
#### How your code is built and packaged for deployment.

---

- **`stacktape-lambda-buildpack`** (recommended): Point to your source file and Stacktape builds,
  bundles, and uploads it automatically.
- **`custom-artifact`**: Provide a pre-built zip file. Stacktape handles the upload.

_MdxDesc.sd: How your code is built and packaged for deployment.
_MdxDesc.ld:
• stacktape-lambda-buildpack (recommended): Point to your source file and Stacktape builds, bundles, and uploads it automatically.
  • custom-artifact: Provide a pre-built zip file. Stacktape handles the upload.

events.description:
#### What triggers this function: HTTP requests, file uploads, queues, schedules, etc.

---

Stacktape auto-configures permissions for each trigger.
The event payload your function receives depends on the trigger type.

_MdxDesc.sd: What triggers this function: HTTP requests, file uploads, queues, schedules, etc.
_MdxDesc.ld: Stacktape auto-configures permissions for each trigger. The event payload your function receives depends on the trigger type.

environment.description:
#### Environment variables available to the function at runtime.

---

Variables from `connectTo` (e.g., `STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING`) are added automatically.

_MdxDesc.sd: Environment variables available to the function at runtime.
_MdxDesc.ld: Variables from connectTo (e.g., STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING) are added automatically.

runtime.description:
#### The language runtime (e.g., `nodejs22.x`, `python3.13`).

---

Auto-detected from your source file extension when using `stacktape-lambda-buildpack`.
Override only if you need a specific version.

_MdxDesc.sd: The language runtime (e.g., nodejs22.x, python3.13).
_MdxDesc.ld: Auto-detected from your source file extension when using stacktape-lambda-buildpack. Override only if you need a specific version.

architecture.description:
#### Processor architecture: `x86_64` (default) or `arm64` (Graviton, ~20% cheaper).

---

`arm64` is cheaper per GB-second and often faster. Works with most code out of the box.
If using `stacktape-lambda-buildpack`, Stacktape builds for the selected architecture automatically.
With `custom-artifact`, you must pre-compile for the target architecture.

_MdxDesc.sd: Processor architecture: x86_64 (default) or arm64 (Graviton, ~20% cheaper).
_MdxDesc.ld: arm64 is cheaper per GB-second and often faster. Works with most code out of the box. If using stacktape-lambda-buildpack, Stacktape builds for the selected architecture automatically. With custom-artifact, you must pre-compile for the target architecture.

memory.description:
#### Memory in MB (128 - 10,240). Also determines CPU power.

---

Lambda scales CPU proportionally to memory: 1,769 MB = 1 vCPU, 3,538 MB = 2 vCPUs, etc.
If your function is slow, increasing memory gives it more CPU, which often makes it faster
and cheaper overall (less execution time).

_MdxDesc.sd: Memory in MB (128 - 10,240). Also determines CPU power.
_MdxDesc.ld: Lambda scales CPU proportionally to memory: 1,769 MB = 1 vCPU, 3,538 MB = 2 vCPUs, etc. If your function is slow, increasing memory gives it more CPU, which often makes it faster and cheaper overall (less execution time).

timeout.description:
#### Max execution time in seconds. Function is killed if it exceeds this.

---

Maximum: 900 seconds (15 minutes). For longer tasks, use a `batch-job` or `worker-service`.

_MdxDesc.sd: Max execution time in seconds. Function is killed if it exceeds this.
_MdxDesc.ld: Maximum: 900 seconds (15 minutes). For longer tasks, use a batch-job or worker-service.

joinDefaultVpc.description:
#### Connects the function to your VPC so it can reach databases, Redis, and other VPC-only resources.

---

**You usually don't need to set this manually.** Stacktape will tell you if a resource in your `connectTo`
requires it (e.g., a database with `accessibilityMode: 'vpc'`, or any Redis cluster).

**Tradeoff:** The function loses direct internet access. It can still reach S3 and DynamoDB
(Stacktape auto-creates VPC endpoints), but calls to external APIs (Stripe, OpenAI, etc.) will fail.
If you need both VPC access and internet, use a `web-service` or `worker-service` instead.

Required when using `volumeMounts` (EFS).

_MdxDesc.sd: Connects the function to your VPC so it can reach databases, Redis, and other VPC-only resources.
_MdxDesc.ld:
You usually don't need to set this manually. Stacktape will tell you if a resource in your connectTo requires it (e.g., a database with accessibilityMode: 'vpc', or any Redis cluster).

Tradeoff: The function loses direct internet access. It can still reach S3 and DynamoDB (Stacktape auto-creates VPC endpoints), but calls to external APIs (Stripe, OpenAI, etc.) will fail. If you need both VPC access and internet, use a web-service or worker-service instead.

Required when using volumeMounts (EFS).

tags.description: #### Additional tags for this function (on top of stack-level tags). Max 50.

_MdxDesc.sd: Additional tags for this function (on top of stack-level tags). Max 50.

destinations.description:
#### Route async invocation results to another service (SQS, SNS, EventBus, or another function).

---

Useful for building event-driven workflows: send successful results to one destination
and failures to another for error handling.

_MdxDesc.sd: Route async invocation results to another service (SQS, SNS, EventBus, or another function).
_MdxDesc.ld: Useful for building event-driven workflows: send successful results to one destination and failures to another for error handling.

logging.description:
#### Logging configuration (retention, forwarding).

---

Logs (`stdout`/`stderr`) are auto-sent to CloudWatch. View with `stacktape logs` or in the Stacktape Console.

_MdxDesc.sd: Logging configuration (retention, forwarding).
_MdxDesc.ld: Logs (stdout/stderr) are auto-sent to CloudWatch. View with stacktape logs or in the Stacktape Console.

provisionedConcurrency.description:
#### Eliminates cold starts by keeping function instances warm and ready.

---

When a function hasn't been called recently, the first request can take 1-5+ seconds ("cold start").
This setting pre-warms the specified number of instances so they respond instantly.

**When to use:** User-facing APIs, web/mobile backends, or any function where response time matters.
Skip this for background jobs, cron tasks, or data pipelines.

**Cost:** You pay for each provisioned instance even when idle. Also increases deploy time by ~2-5 minutes.

_MdxDesc.sd: Eliminates cold starts by keeping function instances warm and ready.
_MdxDesc.ld:
When a function hasn't been called recently, the first request can take 1-5+ seconds ("cold start"). This setting pre-warms the specified number of instances so they respond instantly.

When to use: User-facing APIs, web/mobile backends, or any function where response time matters. Skip this for background jobs, cron tasks, or data pipelines.

Cost: You pay for each provisioned instance even when idle. Also increases deploy time by ~2-5 minutes.

reservedConcurrency.description:
#### Cap the maximum number of concurrent instances for this function.

---

Reserves this many execution slots exclusively for this function — other functions can't use them,
and this function can't scale beyond it. **No additional cost.**

Common uses:
- Prevent overwhelming a database with too many connections
- Guarantee capacity for critical functions
- Throttle expensive downstream API calls

_MdxDesc.sd: Cap the maximum number of concurrent instances for this function.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Reserves this many execution slots exclusively for this function — other functions can't use them, and this function can't scale beyond it. No additional cost.

Common uses:

  • Prevent overwhelming a database with too many connections
  • Guarantee capacity for critical functions
  • Throttle expensive downstream API calls

layers.description:
#### Lambda Layer ARNs to attach (shared libraries, custom runtimes, etc.).

---

Layers are zip archives with additional code/data mounted into the function.
Provide the layer ARN (e.g., from AWS console or another stack). Max 5 layers per function.

_MdxDesc.sd: Lambda Layer ARNs to attach (shared libraries, custom runtimes, etc.).
_MdxDesc.ld: Layers are zip archives with additional code/data mounted into the function. Provide the layer ARN (e.g., from AWS console or another stack). Max 5 layers per function.

deployment.description:
#### Gradual traffic shifting for safe deployments.

---

Instead of switching all traffic to the new version instantly, shift it gradually
(canary or linear). If issues arise, traffic rolls back automatically.

_MdxDesc.sd: Gradual traffic shifting for safe deployments.
_MdxDesc.ld: Instead of switching all traffic to the new version instantly, shift it gradually (canary or linear). If issues arise, traffic rolls back automatically.

_MdxDesc.sd: Alarms for this function (merged with global alarms from the Stacktape Console).

disabledGlobalAlarms.description: #### Global alarm names to exclude from this function.

url.description:

---

Simpler and cheaper than an API Gateway for single-function endpoints.
URL format: `https://{id}.lambda-url.{region}.on.aws`

cdn.description:
#### Put a CDN (CloudFront) in front of this function for caching and lower latency.

---

Caches responses at edge locations worldwide. Reduces function invocations and bandwidth costs.

_MdxDesc.ld: Caches responses at edge locations worldwide. Reduces function invocations and bandwidth costs.

storage.description:
#### Size of the `/tmp` directory in MB (512 - 10,240). Ephemeral per invocation.

---

Increase if your function downloads/processes large files temporarily.

volumeMounts.description:
#### Persistent EFS storage shared across invocations and functions.

---

Unlike `/tmp`, EFS data persists indefinitely and can be shared across multiple functions.
Requires `joinDefaultVpc: true` (Stacktape will remind you if you forget).

_MdxDesc.sd: Persistent EFS storage shared across invocations and functions.
_MdxDesc.ld: Unlike /tmp, EFS data persists indefinitely and can be shared across multiple functions. Requires joinDefaultVpc: true (Stacktape will remind you if you forget).

connectTo.description:
#### Give this resource access to other resources in your stack.

---

List the names of resources this workload needs to communicate with. Stacktape automatically:
- **Grants IAM permissions** (e.g., S3 read/write, SQS send/receive)
- **Opens network access** (security group rules for databases, Redis)
- **Injects environment variables** with connection details: `STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[PARAM]`

Example: `connectTo: ["myDatabase", "myBucket"]` gives this workload full access to both
resources and injects `STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING`, `STP_MY_BUCKET_NAME`, etc.

---

#### What each resource type provides:

**`Bucket`** — read/write/delete objects → `NAME`, `ARN`

**`DynamoDbTable`** — CRUD + scan/query → `NAME`, `ARN`, `STREAM_ARN`

**`RelationalDatabase`** — network access + connection details → `CONNECTION_STRING`, `HOST`, `PORT`.
Aurora also gets `READER_CONNECTION_STRING`, `READER_HOST`.

**`MongoDbAtlasCluster`** — temporary credential-less access → `CONNECTION_STRING`

**`RedisCluster`** — network access → `HOST`, `READER_HOST`, `PORT`

**`Function`** — invoke permission → `ARN`

**`BatchJob`** — submit/list/terminate → `JOB_DEFINITION_ARN`, `STATE_MACHINE_ARN`

**`EventBus`** — publish events → `ARN`

**`UserAuthPool`** — full control → `ID`, `CLIENT_ID`, `ARN`

**`SqsQueue`** — send/receive/delete → `ARN`, `NAME`, `URL`

**`SnsTopic`** — publish/subscribe → `ARN`, `NAME`

**`UpstashRedis`** → `HOST`, `PORT`, `PASSWORD`, `REST_TOKEN`, `REST_URL`, `REDIS_URL`

**`PrivateService`** → `ADDRESS`

**`aws:ses`** — full SES email sending permissions

_MdxDesc.sd: Give this resource access to other resources in your stack.
_MdxDesc.ld:
List the names of resources this workload needs to communicate with. Stacktape automatically:

  • Grants IAM permissions (e.g., S3 read/write, SQS send/receive)
  • Opens network access (security group rules for databases, Redis)
  • Injects environment variables with connection details: STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[PARAM]

Example: connectTo: ["myDatabase", "myBucket"] gives this workload full access to both resources and injects STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING, STP_MY_BUCKET_NAME, etc.

iamRoleStatements.description:
#### Raw IAM policy statements for permissions not covered by `connectTo`.

---

Added as a separate policy alongside auto-generated permissions. Use this for
accessing AWS services directly (e.g., Rekognition, Textract, Bedrock).

_MdxDesc.sd: Raw IAM policy statements for permissions not covered by connectTo.
_MdxDesc.ld: Added as a separate policy alongside auto-generated permissions. Use this for accessing AWS services directly (e.g., Rekognition, Textract, Bedrock).

The function will be invoked with up to this many records. Maximum is 10,000.

---

The function will be triggered when either the `batchSize` is reached or this time window expires.
Maximum is 300 seconds.

#### The authentication method for connecting to the Kafka cluster.

---

- `SASL`: Authenticate using a username and password (PLAIN or SCRAM).
- `MTLS`: Authenticate using a client-side TLS certificate.

_MdxDesc.sd: The authentication method for connecting to the Kafka cluster.
_MdxDesc.ld:
• SASL: Authenticate using a username and password (PLAIN or SCRAM).
  • MTLS: Authenticate using a client-side TLS certificate.

---

- `BASIC_AUTH`: SASL/PLAIN

- `SASL_SCRAM_512_AUTH`: SASL SCRAM-512

You can create secrets using the `stacktape secret:create` command.

strategy.description:
#### How traffic shifts from the old version to the new one.

---

- **Canary**: Send 10% of traffic first, then all traffic after a wait period.
- **Linear**: Shift 10% of traffic at regular intervals.
- **AllAtOnce**: Instant switch (no gradual rollout).

_MdxDesc.sd: How traffic shifts from the old version to the new one.
_MdxDesc.ld:
• Canary: Send 10% of traffic first, then all traffic after a wait period.
  • Linear: Shift 10% of traffic at regular intervals.
  • AllAtOnce: Instant switch (no gradual rollout).

Must signal success/failure to CodeDeploy. If it fails, the deployment rolls back.

afterTrafficShiftFunction.description:

the threshold to fire the alarm. Useful for filtering out short spikes.

Enable the function URL.

---

- `NONE` — public, anyone can call it.
- `AWS_IAM` — only authenticated AWS users/roles with invoke permission.

---

Improves Time to First Byte and increases max response size from 6 MB to 20 MB.
Requires using the AWS streaming handler pattern in your code.

_MdxDesc.ld: Improves Time to First Byte and increases max response size from 6 MB to 20 MB. Requires using the AWS streaming handler pattern in your code.

EdgeLambdaFunction.description:
#### Lambda function that runs at CDN edge locations for request/response manipulation.

---

Runs on CloudFront events (viewer request, origin request, etc.) to modify headers, rewrite URLs,
implement A/B testing, or add auth checks at the edge. Referenced from CDN `edgeFunctions` config.

overrides.description:
#### Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.

---

Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource.
Find resource logical IDs with `stacktape stack-info --detailed`.

```yaml
overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true
```

_MdxDesc.sd: Escape hatch to modify the underlying CloudFormation resources Stacktape creates.
_MdxDesc.ld:
Use dot-notation paths to override specific properties on any child resource. Find resource logical IDs with stacktape stack-info --detailed.

overrides:
  MyDbInstance:
    Properties.StorageEncrypted: true

_MdxDesc.ld: Runs on CloudFront events (viewer request, origin request, etc.) to modify headers, rewrite URLs, implement A/B testing, or add auth checks at the edge. Referenced from CDN edgeFunctions config.

connectTo.description:
#### Give this resource access to other resources in your stack.

---

List the names of resources this workload needs to communicate with. Stacktape automatically:
- **Grants IAM permissions** (e.g., S3 read/write, SQS send/receive)
- **Opens network access** (security group rules for databases, Redis)
- **Injects environment variables** with connection details: `STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[PARAM]`

Example: `connectTo: ["myDatabase", "myBucket"]` gives this workload full access to both
resources and injects `STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING`, `STP_MY_BUCKET_NAME`, etc.

---

#### What each resource type provides:

**`Bucket`** — read/write/delete objects → `NAME`, `ARN`

**`DynamoDbTable`** — CRUD + scan/query → `NAME`, `ARN`, `STREAM_ARN`

**`RelationalDatabase`** — network access + connection details → `CONNECTION_STRING`, `HOST`, `PORT`.
Aurora also gets `READER_CONNECTION_STRING`, `READER_HOST`.

**`MongoDbAtlasCluster`** — temporary credential-less access → `CONNECTION_STRING`

**`RedisCluster`** — network access → `HOST`, `READER_HOST`, `PORT`

**`Function`** — invoke permission → `ARN`

**`BatchJob`** — submit/list/terminate → `JOB_DEFINITION_ARN`, `STATE_MACHINE_ARN`

**`EventBus`** — publish events → `ARN`

**`UserAuthPool`** — full control → `ID`, `CLIENT_ID`, `ARN`

**`SqsQueue`** — send/receive/delete → `ARN`, `NAME`, `URL`

**`SnsTopic`** — publish/subscribe → `ARN`, `NAME`

**`UpstashRedis`** → `HOST`, `PORT`, `PASSWORD`, `REST_TOKEN`, `REST_URL`, `REDIS_URL`

**`PrivateService`** → `ADDRESS`

**`aws:ses`** — full SES email sending permissions

_MdxDesc.sd: Give this resource access to other resources in your stack.
_MdxDesc.ld:
List the names of resources this workload needs to communicate with. Stacktape automatically:

  • Grants IAM permissions (e.g., S3 read/write, SQS send/receive)
  • Opens network access (security group rules for databases, Redis)
  • Injects environment variables with connection details: STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[PARAM]

Example: connectTo: ["myDatabase", "myBucket"] gives this workload full access to both resources and injects STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING, STP_MY_BUCKET_NAME, etc.

#### Give this resource access to other resources in your stack.

---

List the names of resources this workload needs to communicate with. Stacktape automatically:
- **Grants IAM permissions** (e.g., S3 read/write, SQS send/receive)
- **Opens network access** (security group rules for databases, Redis)
- **Injects environment variables** with connection details: `STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[PARAM]`

Example: `connectTo: ["myDatabase", "myBucket"]` gives this workload full access to both
resources and injects `STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING`, `STP_MY_BUCKET_NAME`, etc.

---

#### What each resource type provides:

**`Bucket`** — read/write/delete objects → `NAME`, `ARN`

**`DynamoDbTable`** — CRUD + scan/query → `NAME`, `ARN`, `STREAM_ARN`

**`RelationalDatabase`** — network access + connection details → `CONNECTION_STRING`, `HOST`, `PORT`.
Aurora also gets `READER_CONNECTION_STRING`, `READER_HOST`.

**`MongoDbAtlasCluster`** — temporary credential-less access → `CONNECTION_STRING`

**`RedisCluster`** — network access → `HOST`, `READER_HOST`, `PORT`

**`Function`** — invoke permission → `ARN`

**`BatchJob`** — submit/list/terminate → `JOB_DEFINITION_ARN`, `STATE_MACHINE_ARN`

**`EventBus`** — publish events → `ARN`

**`UserAuthPool`** — full control → `ID`, `CLIENT_ID`, `ARN`

**`SqsQueue`** — send/receive/delete → `ARN`, `NAME`, `URL`

**`SnsTopic`** — publish/subscribe → `ARN`, `NAME`

**`UpstashRedis`** → `HOST`, `PORT`, `PASSWORD`, `REST_TOKEN`, `REST_URL`, `REDIS_URL`

**`PrivateService`** → `ADDRESS`

**`aws:ses`** — full SES email sending permissions

_MdxDesc.sd: Give this resource access to other resources in your stack.
_MdxDesc.ld:
List the names of resources this workload needs to communicate with. Stacktape automatically:

  • Grants IAM permissions (e.g., S3 read/write, SQS send/receive)
  • Opens network access (security group rules for databases, Redis)
  • Injects environment variables with connection details: STP_[RESOURCE_NAME]_[PARAM]

Example: connectTo: ["myDatabase", "myBucket"] gives this workload full access to both resources and injects STP_MY_DATABASE_CONNECTION_STRING, STP_MY_BUCKET_NAME, etc.

iamRoleStatements.description:
#### Raw IAM policy statements for permissions not covered by `connectTo`.

---

Added as a separate policy alongside auto-generated permissions. Use this for
accessing AWS services directly (e.g., Rekognition, Textract, Bedrock).
